One of very few Latin American countries w/ **Gen2 coverage**

shared_with.note: Brazil has some Gen2 but mostly overwritten.

hints.text: Gen3 cars: **white w/ stubby coiled antenna** OR white w/ visible back + **long red brake light** (rarely just a sliver where the antenna sits); Gen4 cars often show a front — most common **blue**, **white w/ blue stripe**, or **black**
hints.uniq_note: The stubby coiled antenna also appears in US national parks — ALTO/Spanish decides.

hints.text: **Area codes**: first digit = region (map); **postal codes** at the bottom of street signs — ordered alphabetically by state name (map)
Every state has **unique plate designs** — the most useful ones are learnable through the blur (infographic)
**Vegetation by region**:
bullets: Prickly pear (flat pads): central Mexico, rare south/coastal
Pulque agave (wide curling crown): central valley + around San Luis Potosí
Blue agave fields (tequila): Jalisco & Nayarit
Tree yucca: San Luis Potosí, Zacatecas + northern neighbours
Mexican Giant Cardon (huge sprawling arms): Baja peninsula
Saguaro (short curved arms): western Sonora
Organ Pipe cactus (thin stems from ground level): Sonora, some BCS
Blue Myrtle cactus (short up-curving arms): SLP, Querétaro, Hidalgo
Sugar cane: everywhere but by far most Veracruz
Coconut palms in big groups: Guerrero & Colima
Sabal palms (spiky sun crown): east coast, Yucatán, Nayarit
Attalea rostrata (upright umbrella palm): Veracruz/Tabasco
Oil-palm plantations: Chiapas (+ edges of Veracruz/Tabasco/Campeche)
Boojum trees (alien tentacles): southern half of Baja
Brittlebush (silver-grey shrub): Sonora, esp. Hermosillo
Thin white-barked trees: Quintana Roo/Yucatán
Gold grass tufts lining the road: Coahuila; teardrop grass clumps: Tlaxcala

hints.text: **Landscapes**: red fields → Zacatecas (+S-Durango); Monterrey ringed by tall mountains; stunted-pine rocky mountains → W-Chihuahua/Durango; extremely dark fields → Guanajuato; reddish hills w/ boulders → N-Baja; **blindingly white soil → Quintana Roo** (Gen3 only); red fields in dense tropical forest → N-Campeche

**Architecture**: red brick → west of CDMX (Jalisco/Michoacán/Guanajuato/Aguascalientes); white concrete blocks → around CDMX & east; mud brick → Durango + desert states; northern Mexico = US-bungalow style w/ pastel colours, gates, front yards, Spanish-colonial orange tiles; thatched roofs → Yucatán + E-SLP; light rock walls → Yucatán state
**State infrastructure metas**:
bullets: Thick cross on sign backs → Chihuahua
Acorn-shaped white insulators → coasts (Michoacán, Colima, Veracruz)
Asymmetric crossbar + double-stacked insulators → CDMX/Edomex/Hidalgo/Morelos
Lowered crossbar wooden poles → Sinaloa & Sonora
Concrete poles w/ insulators HANGING below crossbars → Yucatán peninsula
Wooden hanging-insulator poles → Baja peninsula
Lowered crossbar + single support beam → Durango
Mesh-body poles → SLP, Guanajuato, Querétaro
White middle lines → Tabasco
Red-bark tree fences → Veracruz (+ other coastal states)
Black tar beside the yellow centre line → Sinaloa/Durango/Guerrero
Grainy blue-grey pavement → Tamaulipas & Nuevo León (Tamaulipas: dashed outer lines)
Irregular cobblestone → Nayarit/Jalisco/Colima/Querétaro
Hexagonal-tile pavement → Puebla & Tlaxcala
Three-line cobblestone pattern → Oaxaca
Taxis white w/ pink top → Mexico City

hints.text: Jalisco bollards sometimes carry a **crown + 'Jalisco' text** — no other Mexican bollard has writing
Bollards w/ a **red band near the top** — unique to Nayarit (one exception: Federal Hwy 190 on the Oaxaca-Chiapas border)
hints.uniq_note: Remember the Hwy-190 exception before locking Nayarit.

hints.text: **City lamp metas**: Aguascalientes = yellow lamps; Cuernavaca = black rectangular head + green-painted pole bottoms (faded in Gen4); Santiago de Querétaro = black edges + white middle; Cancún = five-bulb 'dice' lamp; Matamoros = ring-bulb; Hermosillo = zig-zag arm; Chihuahua city = white pole strip w/ street name
**Spotlights**:
bullets: Mexicali: flat farmland, ridge west, Washingtonia palms + brown wooden poles
Poza Rica (Veracruz): citrus plantations on hilltops
Ciudad Obregón: pinkish-tan soil + irrigation canals
Twin volcanoes Iztaccíhuatl & Popocatépetl: Edomex/Puebla border (usually faint)
Cerro del Bernal: lone extinct volcano, S-Tamaulipas
Sierra del Abra Tanchipa: extremely long flat ridge, E-SLP
Maderas del Carmen: tall flat ridge, N-Coahuila (only visible from Hwy 53)
Ciudad Juárez outskirts: orange dirt roads + low shrubs
Suchixtlahuaca (Oaxaca): hills alternating orange/beige soil
Guaymas: jagged cliffs, pinkish soil, organ-pipe cacti
Las Coloradas: pink lakes
Hwy 54 (NE-Zacatecas): yucca spam both sides + overexposed white car
40D (Sinaloa–Durango): lush cliffs, tunnels, double yellow lines
95D (CDMX→Acapulco): divider w/ yellow top & bottom edge
Eurus wind farm (SE-Oaxaca, Hwy 190): Mexico's largest turbine rows

hints.text: **Special cars**: yellow-hood car → La Malinche volcano (Tlaxcala); white truck w/ visible mirrors → Cofre de Perote NP; red car w/ black roof → La Seca (Sinaloa)
Microstate on the **French Riviera**, a few km west of the Italian border; rich, densely populated city state — essentially **no rural areas**
Architecture like southern France / northern Italy but **more luxurious**: apartment towers, neoclassical details, pastel colours

shared_with.note: Riviera cities like Nice look similar.
shared_with.diff: Short strip-less plates + extreme density/luxury → Monaco.

hints.text: Monégasque plates **short & without the blue strip** (many normal French plates around too — often old yellow rears, the Gen2 coverage is that old)
hints.uniq_note: Short + no EU strip separates from France; but French-plated cars dominate the streets, so look for several.

hints.text: **Hilly**; the few unbuilt spots show Mediterranean vegetation; planted **palm trees** common
A lot of coverage **inside tunnels**
Rows of **parked mopeds** all around the streets
**Harbour Gen3 boat trekker**: brown-haired Google employee w/ sunglasses, expensive yachts, skyline + hills behind — appears a lot in competitive modes, worth memorising
Plates mostly white with a **red Soyombo hint** on the left
**Mongolian Cyrillic** with **Ө Ү** (not in Russian) + frequent **double vowels** (аа, өө, ээ); Latin & traditional Mongolian script occasionally

shared_with.diff: **Ө Ү** or double vowels → Mongolia, plus the pickup camera & steppe.

shared_with.note: Kyrgyz has Ө Ү (and Ң) too.
shared_with.diff: Ң or winter Gen3 silver car → Kyrgyzstan; double vowels → Mongolia.

shared_with.note: Kazakh has Ө Ү (and more extras).
shared_with.diff: **Ә Қ Ғ Ұ І** → Kazakhstan; white KZ pickup vs Mongolian truck w/ tent.

hints.text: All coverage driven with a **large pickup truck** — often visible, with tent, red side mirrors, snorkel or roof racks
hints.uniq_note: Other countries have pickup cameras — but the tent/snorkel/red-mirror truck combos in an empty steppe are unmistakably Mongolia.

hints.text: **Steppe biomes**: empty grasslands/deserts with characteristic **lack of trees**; rugged/rocky/rolling hills, rivers & lakes
White **bowling-pin bollards with two red stripes** near the top
Russian-influenced infra: wooden poles w/ stone support blocks, white lamp posts, 3-stripe ped signs; **ЗОГС stop sign** is unique; each town has its own lamp-post design
hints.uniq_note: The ЗОГС stop sign exists nowhere else; the rest of the infrastructure is shared post-Soviet style (Russia etc.) — use the stop sign or the camera truck to confirm.

hints.text: Neighbourhoods separated by **dirt alleys + wooden fences**; white round **yurts** everywhere; large **animal herds** (horses/yaks/camels…); **ovoos** (decorated stone piles/shrines)
**Coverage very limited** — highways + big towns, densest around Ulaanbaatar (lots of hidden coverage exists); driving direction is usually **away from Ulaanbaatar**
**Topography**: flat east & south · rolling hills north · big mountains/rocky hills west
bullets: Forests (larch/pine/birch) in grassy fields → north
Dirt tracks in flat vast grass → far east
Off-road desert → south/east of Mandalgovi
Arid steppe rangeland (coarse soil, patchy grass) → southwest
Dry rugged hills/mountains → far west; lakes & rivers → west
Snow coverage → mostly around Ulaanbaatar

hints.text: **Fall coverage** (orange trees/grass) only Lun↔Hatgal + some Ulaanbaatar; **Bugant forest**: narrow Russian-looking dirt trails w/ close trees + birches (trails not on the map); **crop fields** rare — Selenge & Töv near UB
By far the biggest city (~50% of the population): high-rises, chains, buses, pedestrians, clean roads; suburbs = vast **yurt districts** on hills
**Highway metas**
bullets: **Choir Highway** (Nalaikh→Zamiin-Uud): longest paved desert road — dry brown, clear skies, flat, **railroad alongside** (west of it N of Sainshand, east S of it); tentless car
**Mandalgovi desert highway**: cloudy skies, darker pavement, **deep blue tent**
**Mandalgovi Highway** S of UB: covered in a **blizzard** — extremely snowy/foggy, deep blue tent, flat
**Millennium Road** W of Lun: late fall, orange short grass, **silver tent**, sun sets toward the west end (continues off-road to Murun)
**Darkhan–Erdenet**: rolling hills, tall transmission towers, crops; mostly winter w/ tentless car, light snow (summer patches east)
**Chinggis–Baruun-Urt**: only paved eastern highway — cloudy, fresh dark pavement, flat
**Road to Khuder** (north): railroad along nearly the whole road (not on map), river south in the west half

hints.text: **Gen3 car metas** (split the country by the truck)
bullets: Roof racks: **rubber inlays = west**, empty racks = east
Spare tire (back right) splits 4 zones: netted / single strap / no strap / no tire
East grasslands: **light-blue tent + forward snorkel**; orange roof-rack strap layout subdivides the east; **red tensioner straps → Chinggis** area
North of UB: blue tent + **backwards snorkel** (+rolling hills/forests)
Tentless car: NW diagonal through UB (on Choir hwy w/ double left mirror + backwards snorkel); always drives AWAY from UB
**Silver tent** → central & northwest
**Grey tent w/ hooks** → west (usually netted spare); hooks angled down → southwest; no hooks left + billowing → Khovd–Ölgii road/Tugrug
**Cage on the roof** (winter, snowy) → near UB; green camo sleeping bag on the cage → west of UB

hints.text: **Gen4 car metas**: UB = silver truck w/ ornate **blue-white roof decal** + snorkel/antenna; east = silver pickup w/ **metal box + shovel** (Bodi Group); west = silver pickup w/ big **light-blue tent** — strap variants: 1 knot = NW, knots both straps = SW, clumped lower strap = central-south (UB→Altai), 4 straps = central-north, open zipper = near Khorgo
**Town spotlights (north/centre)**
bullets: **Erdenet** (2nd city): multistorey buildings, stadium, ferris wheel S; barren hills N+S, cloudy winter, no snow (vs Darkhan), no rubber inlays (vs UB)
**Darkhan** (3rd city): the only big city with **snow coverage** (besides UB suburbs)
**Sukhbaatar & Altanbulag** (Russian border): dry forested hills + heavy overcast (Sukhbaatar fully overcast)
**Murun**: N-S grid, jagged hill north, muddy alleys, dark cloudy (sometimes sunny)
**Hatgal**: lake town on Khuvsgul — fall colours + big lake NE
**Tosontsengel**: red-tinted roads + unique wavy lamp posts
**Tsetserleg**: rocky rugged hills w/ small trees to the N, very clear skies
**Arvaikheer**: warm green grassy Gen4, rolling hills all around, ferris wheel (old coverage: right-side smudge)
**Bayankhongor**: dry; big hill east w/ **Soyombo symbol** (old coverage: right-side smudge)
**Uliastai**: green hills, river through town, Soyombo hill in the centre

hints.text: **Town spotlights (east/south/west)**
bullets: **Chinggis**: dry & flat, small hills N, red tent straps (only Dundburd/Bayan-Ovoo share them — unpaved villages)
**Choibalsan**: only major city with **concrete roads**; smoke stack SE, wet-after-rain overcast; odd ped signs (short figure over 3 stripes)
**Baruun-Urt**: towering construction site w/ orange crane ('Smile' POI), dry
**Khalkhgol** (far east): flat green + giant **sword statue**
**Dariganga**: lone hill NW of town
**Sainshand**: biggest desert town; dry ridge splits N/S; mini pink **Eiffel tower** downtown; white lamps
**Zamiin-Uud** (China border): **sailboat lamp posts** w/ golden flower pots + folded-script signs (Choir = blue lamps)
**Mandalgovi**: big desert town, cloudy, deep blue tent
**Dalanzadgad**: **sunset coverage**, fall grass, rocky hills S, loose left tent cover
**Altai**: dark hill ranges S+W, wet roads after rain (old coverage: left-inflated tent)
**Khovd**: dry shrubby, distinct **red-tinted rocky hills** north
**Ölgii**: close dry rocky mountains + red-white **radio tower** in the south
**Ulaangom**: big dark mountains SW + **double lamp posts** w/ blue/green base

hints.text: **Landscape spotlights**: far-west glacier peaks (tallest mountains; car w/ green/black bag over right racks); shrubs+patchy grass E of Uvs lake (Shaazgai similar but hillier); **Khan Khokhii NP** — densest forests, dark soil, grey tent; **Gobi Gurvan Saikhan NP** — bright orange dunes (Jeep follow car in the SW corner); **Yolyn Am valley** — extreme fog + dark soil; **Khoridol Saridag** river valley — rocky cliffs + pebble-covered ground

**Night coverage** in exactly 4 areas: NW of Murun, W of Dashbalbar, S of Baruun-Urt, E of Dariganga — always driving toward town; **rainstorm coverage** E of Khorgo (dark skies, raindrops on camera, sometimes a rainbow)
Plates: EU blue strip + the **coat of arms on a red circle** — the red circle stays visible through the blur

shared_with.note: The white section of Albanian plates has no red circle.
shared_with.diff: Red circle in the blur → Montenegro.

hints.text: All Gen4 = **smallcam**: low-mounted camera, big circular blur w/ small front protrusion (can be fully transparent)

shared_with.note: Most of the Balkans and southern Europe have smallcam.
shared_with.diff: Smallcam only narrows to the region.

hints.text: **Rifts** (sky tears) uncommonly in Gen3

shared_with.note: Rifts are also common in Albania; rarely in various other countries.

hints.text: Small, **mountainous** country: inland mountains lush & green, coastal mountains w/ **exposed light-coloured rock**; roads mostly 1–2 lanes
Most common bollard: white w/ **red front / white back reflector + black top**

shared_with.note: These bollards are also found in Slovenia.
shared_with.diff: Rusted guardrails + karst coast → Montenegro; Alpine look → Slovenia.

hints.text: Chevrons **black-on-white or red-on-yellow**
Guardrails often **rusted**; only Balkan country where **both A- and B-type** guardrails are common
hints.uniq_note: Within the Balkans the A+B mix is a Montenegro tell; single guardrail sightings prove nothing.

hints.text: Simple **round concrete or wooden poles**
Gen3 rift map: **coast = short antenna, no rifts** / **inland = long antenna + rifts** (exception: around Utjeha & Dobra Voda on the coast)
Green agricultural valleys w/ deforested hills → **north**; dry treeless mountains + vineyards → around/south of **Podgorica**; pine forests → north, N of Nikšić, most commonly **east**
**Bay of Kotor**: touristy, populated, swimwear + a distinct steep rocky ridge; **Durmitor NP**: grassy treeless mountains w/ high-altitude feel (town of Žabljak inside, unique architecture)
**Podgorica** = capital & largest city: modern buildings, wide roads, modern infrastructure

**Spotlights**: staircase-like mountainsides → Cemi River Valley; many small islands → Slansko Lake; wide artificial gravel wall → Pljevlja Coal Mine (visible from afar); railway + stone bollards → Skadar Lake Bridge
Plates **yellow front & back** (vanity plates = white w/ blue text) — only Botswana & Namibia predominantly use yellow rear plates
Drives **left**; all coverage = white pickup w/ a **short front antenna that leans slightly left** (Oman's leans right)
Roads: single **yellow outer + white centre** lines (like the rest of Southern Africa)
Green direction signs w/ yellow road numbers; roads use **A/B/C/D/M (+ F)** — South Africa uses N & R
Former **German colony** → German place names + some German-style old buildings; small stone bollards at intersections; green/white round bins at rest stops
Coverage mostly main roads; **area codes** narrow urban rounds; where the **car is blurred** correlates with region (2024 coverage)
**D-road first digit = region**; 3-digit numbers (D203) in the far south → 4-digit (1/2/3) further north
East of Windhoek = Botswana-like flat/red; NW greener; **coastal sand dunes** around Swakopmund; tall **pink dunes** east of Sossusvlei; lush green → S of Tsumeb
Plates fairly long, most commonly white; **private = red, public = black**
ALL road coverage = **shitcam** (brownish, big circular blur)

shared_with.note: Shitcam is very common in India too.
shared_with.diff: India also has smallcam Gen4 + trekkers; Nepali Devanagari uses **ङ/ङ्ग** and native Devanagari numerals (rare in India).

hints.text: Drives on the **left**
**Devanagari** (top line, straight verticals) — Nepali tells: **ङ / ङ्ग** much more common than in Hindi + native **Devanagari numerals** on signs (very rare in India)

shared_with.diff: ङ्ग + Devanagari numerals + all-shitcam + Himalaya → Nepal.

hints.text: Square concrete poles (flats) / thin metal poles (mountains), often trident tops
bullets: Concrete pole w/ **trident top + triangle supports** = unique to Nepal
Square pole w/ **2 vertical side insulators, wide gap** = almost unique
Other poles can look very Indian
hints.uniq_note: The trident+triangle-support pole is unique, the wide-gap insulator pole almost unique — all other Nepali poles look like India's, so anchor on those two.

hints.text: **Outer yellow lines** (dashed/solid) + dashed white middle on bigger roads; solid outer yellow ↔ very common in eastern Bagmati
**Black-white striped concrete bollards** (3–5 stripes), often a red top marking
Flat agriculture in the **south**, high-altitude mountain roads in the **northern half**
Colourful blocky multi-storey buildings or small painted-concrete/brick/thatch houses; **triangle-shaped facade decorations** common
**Black-white striped signposts**
White **Buddhist stupas, sometimes with painted eyes** — only in Nepal
**Districts** appear in addresses, often in English too; far south = flat (Terai), centre/north = high mountains

**Vegetation metas**: western hills dry w/ exposed hillsides (east greener); areca palms → southeast; canola → southern pockets in the west; chir pines → mountains (esp. west + near Kathmandu); white fluffy grass → SE of Kathmandu; Japanese cedars + tea plantations → far east only

**Roof metas**: rounded tiles → flats S of Kathmandu + west; West-Bengal-style tiles (diamond protrusions) → far-west flats; grey square stone tiles → Birgunj/Janakpur (NOT in India-lookalike areas!); messy rectangular tiles → western mountain towns; corrugated metal → Birgunj–Janakpur; roof **railings** → west (Tulsipur valley, stair-shaped; east limit ≈ Kawasoti); golden god-carved gables → around Kawasoti; stick fences → eastern plains; triangular roof 'window' + sloping section → far east; hip roofs w/ small ridge protrusion → (west of) Biratnagar
**Mosques** only in Muslim pockets: around Janakpur, Birgunj, Nepalgunj, Biratnagar/W of Koshi; round clay huts w/ thatch → around Birgunj (also across the Indian border)
Poles w/ a **cross near the top** → east; Kathmandu poles: asymmetric crossbar + triangle attachment + support bar; **yellow water tanks** → east (green/black everywhere)

**Car metas**: orange car → Nepalgunj/Madi/Biratnagar/Birtamod (w/ blue ribbon = Biratnagar); blue car → Pokhara/Birgunj/Malangawa (w/ antenna = Pokhara); white car → Butwal, H10, H04, near Kathmandu, NE-Birgunj, Janakpur–Lahan, Itahari; black spoiler → Janakpur; grey car → Kathmandu + Lahan; blocky grey truck → E of Birgunj + S of Kathmandu; grey car w/ backlight → Pokhara/Kathmandu/N-Malangawa/E-Itahari; red car (long antenna) → Kathmandu + N of Janakpur

**Road spotlights**: N of Siddhicharan (east) — high hills full of dark **rhododendron bushes** (red flowers); NW of Bhimeshwor — foggy gloomy road in the only natural **east-Himalayan-fir forest**; NE of Phungling — sketchy yellow/orange dirt road w/ high-elevation oaks; N of Khaptad NP — NW-bound valley dirt track w/ snowcapped mountains NW
**Trekker coverage** only SW of Mount Everest — green forests in the south, barren trails north; near Namche Bazar: sunny near the treeline; compass mostly unreliable!

**Trekker settlements**: Namche Bazar = biggest, dense (+bird's-eye views); Lukla = southernmost, narrow street, extremely dark; Khumjung = jagged peak above + nearly all **green roofs** (label further north is misleading); Pangboche = bright, forested mountains; Phorste = sparse, huge mountain, dead-looking trees
**Trail metas**
bullets: Lukla→Namche: dark overcast forest valley → brighter N of Phakding → sunny Monjo→Namche
Namche→Thame: forested NW-SE valley (Thamu); shining snowy range at Thame
Namche→Gokyo: semi-green river valley in clouds → Tobuche cloud-blanket → smoke-like spot clouds → clear Tobuche → icy lake at Gokyo → barren NNW trail

Gokyo→Lobuche: grey dried **glacial valley** → red rugged mountainside → rising U-valley → spiky range → the country's biggest snow patch (glacier crossing) → irregular clouds → Tobuche from the east → wide high river valley
Namche→Dingboche: overcast mountainside trail high above the valley → bright sandy overcast forest E of Deboche → hazy sun between Pangboche & Dingboche
→ Everest: low-cloud valley N of Dingboche → barren NNE trail → the **view of Mount Everest** at the end

hints.text: Plates long **yellow** w/ blue strip left; taxis = blue plates

shared_with.note: UK rear plates yellow (often no strip).

hints.text: **Dutch**: double vowels, ij/ieuw/oe clusters, capitalised **IJ** as one letter (unique)

shared_with.diff: Belgian white plates w/ red text + utility poles → Belgium.

hints.text: Simple **white bollard w/ red reflector** (rare French-style round ones — red wrap doesn't go all the way around)
**Essentially NO street-level utility poles** (only big power lines)

shared_with.note: Belgium DOES use street poles fairly commonly.

hints.text: **Extraordinarily flat** (exceptions: S-Limburg low hills, De Veluwe); very **water-rich**: canals/ditches beside roads, dikes, dams, bridges; tree-lined rural roads (also Poland)
Highly **planned & organised**: well-kept roads, master-planned row-house suburbs

All-white lines, often **dashed outer**; sometimes unique **green middle lines**; **reddish-pink bicycle lanes** everywhere + cyclists; brick roads (distinct pattern); grey square sidewalk tiles offset-laid
hints.uniq_note: Green middle lines are NL-unique; the rest of the road kit (bike lanes, brick roads) is strongly Dutch but exists in Belgium too.

hints.text: Speed bumps w/ **alternating long/short white stripes**; rural **brick rumble strips** on road sides — fairly NL-unique, great for rural rounds
Street signs **blue w/ white letters** (street = straat; -weg/-laan/-pad/-plein; 'fietspad' = just a bike path!); Belgium: multicoloured + municipality named

shared_with.note: Belgian street signs vary in colour and usually carry the municipality.

hints.text: Direction signs blue/white (bike versions white w/ red border); **blue town-entry signs**, often elaborate, listing 'gemeente' (municipality = scan helper!)
**Green road markers**: red box + A = highway, yellow box + N = regional; sign edges **extend backwards** creating a visible border from behind — consistent & unique to NL
Signposts w/ **b&w or blue-white striped markings**; bulky **yellow posts** on traffic islands; striped rural barriers
**Red-brown brick architecture** (orange or dark-grey tile roofs); **row houses** ≈ half the housing stock — Europe-top; windmills + turbines common
Gen4 glitch showing the **black back of the car**; smallcam coverage

**Area codes** (3–4 digits, geographically grouped except 07x + two 02x); **hectometre markers** on N-roads have **province-specific designs**; bollard km stickers regional; province flags (mostly rare — exceptions: **Friesland, Noord-Brabant**, unofficial Achterhoek)
**Friesland**: bilingual town signs (Frisian); street endings **-wei / -strjitte**; circumflex vowels Â Ê Ô Û + frequent Y
Street-light stickers: **Limburg = vertical 3–4 digits**; **North Brabant = horizontal 1–2 digits**

**West/north = polder** (marshy, many small waterways); south/east = normal flat farmland; S-Limburg = low hills; **Veluwe** = unusually forested, heath + sandy soil ('un-Dutch' look); **Flevoland** = reclaimed 1950s land — dead-straight roads, huge fields, turbines, zero historic buildings (exception: **Urk**, an ex-island town); coast = dunes; **Wadden Islands** = touristy + NW dune coasts
Northern farms = **very large roofs**; **rolling shutters** more common in the south; **Curaçao** counts as NL (own page)
**Amsterdam**: street signs w/ **district below the name**; **Oegstgeest**: illegible yellow-on-red signs; **Lelystad**: streets named neighbourhood+number (on the map!)
**Zaanstreek** (N of Amsterdam): green/black wooden houses w/ white trims + white roof spires; **Baarle**: Belgian enclaves — border markings on the pavement, both flags

**Afsluitdijk**: dead-straight SW-NE divided causeway, water SE + dike NW; **Westland**: greenhouse ocean SW of The Hague; **Rotterdam harbour**: industry + round fuel tanks (Amsterdam/Delfzijl similar); Hoge Veluwe NP trekker w/ visible **brown hair**
Bollard: **red strip wraps around the top** (unique to NZ), thin yellow/white reflector on front or back
Plates white w/ black text (appear mostly white through the blur)
Poles = concrete w/ **one long indent** + small silver **possum guards**; wooden & holey poles also exist
**Give-way signs red text** (Australia = black); **give-way triangles painted on the road** (AU doesn't do this)
Warning signs diamond yellow on **white posts**; **blue street signs** common (esp. rural) — Australia almost never blue
White outer lines; **yellow dashed outer** in towns; inner = single dashed white or **double yellow** (AU has no yellow inner, snow aside)
Chevrons **black-on-yellow with a thin black border** (Australia = same colours, no border)
Green directional signs (state-highway # in a **red crest**); **bridge markers** (yellow = river name); brown signs = landmarks
**Ferns** very common in forested areas
Two-digit **state-highway numbers run N→S**; **Fonterra farm-sign** first digit = region
Phone = 7 digits + 2-digit area code (02 = mobile); city rubbish bins & bus-stop signs identify Auckland/Wellington/Christchurch
**Southern Alps** snowy range (South Island); West Coast temperate **rainforest**; Waikato/Northland dry rolling hills; **Mt Taranaki** smooth cone; teal glacial lakes near Queenstown
Pole stickers & possum-guard styles pinpoint region (Gisborne yellow cow-tag; Marlborough olive-brown guard; Southland cylindrical); Canterbury = tall hedge rows on flat plains
Visible **pickup truck** in most coverage (cab + bed; colours vary; Gen3 has a roof rack; sometimes one huge blur)
A **police follow car** (mostly white or black) almost always trails the Street View car

hints.text: **Guardrails often on wooden posts** (not SE/FI); rusty guardrails → coasts (esp. southern west coast)

hints.text: Pedestrian signs: 4 stripes + **very simply drawn person** (variants: 5 stripes, hatted person); SE = detailed person no hat, FI = 5 stripes

**Farmland triangle**: SE/Oslo = flattest, rolling w/ smooth forested hills; Trondheim = hilly & uneven; south of Stavanger (Jæren) = flat, vast open grass fields + **round-rock walls**

**Deep fjords + tall mountains** → Bergen↔Trondheim; **Lofoten** = sharp rocky low-tree mountains; NE = rolling, tiny birches + patchy grass; SW = barren treeless highlands; south coast = exposed rock w/ coastal pines; far-north coast = bare grasslands + low distant mountains; all-white villages → south coast
**Black snow poles** → Bergen↔Trondheim; small wide black pole plates → southeast; **Svalbard** = separate page here
**Oslo**: big black/red pole plates; **Stavanger**: two red stripes on lamp posts; **Bergen**: green fire hydrants

Private plates **yellow**, commercial **red**

**Vegetation**: acacias (umbrella crowns) → north + coasts; date palms → north coast; coconut palms → Salalah; gas pipelines along roads → around Fahud + central-south pockets; oil drills → Amal, Road 39, NW of Fahud

**Bin metas**: colour map by region; bin **numbers = governorate + subdivision codes**; sticker combos: black+black sticker → Al Buraimi/Ash Sharqiyah N/Muscat; grey+black → Ad Dhahirah; black+white → Ad Dakhiliyah/S-Dhofar/Muscat; grey+white → Ad Dakhiliyah (+Dhofar); black no sticker → Al Batinah S + N of Al Khaburah; grey no sticker → Al Batinah N; small western-style black → Muscat + E-Al-Batinah-N; chevron stickers on the side → Muscat; ANY side sticker → north-coast governorates
**Antenna metas**: upward antenna → around/west of Muscat + NW highways (+Al Buraimi, Sohar); forward-facing antenna → only Muscat area (copyright-year splits exist but update on refresh)

**Spotlights**: Mirbat ridge (more rugged than Salalah's); crown-shaped lone mountain SE of Nizwa; Adam = hazy, ridge+two hills north (north of Adam: mountains all around); Ubar = green farm patches in desert; Al Amarat (SE of Muscat) = urban ringed by mountains; **Fahud** = bumpy ridge + oil infrastructure; **Musandam exclave** = UAE-like extra-dry orange mountains w/ dipping beds; coastal Musandam = rocky cliffs, coast mostly west

Roads 45 / west-39: hilly, sunny, E-W, light shoulders + faded middle line — sun overhead = 39, sun west = 45

bullets: Tumbes off-roads: sandy soil + green/withered bushes — and NEVER a black car (white only)

hints.text: All-white lines; **double middle lines** very common — good Poland clue

hints.text: Direction signs **green** w/ distinct Polish font (expressways blue); town entry: green name sign + b&w stylised-town sign; chevrons white w/ red arrows

**Polish**: Ł (unique), Ą/Ę hooks (also Lithuanian), Ć Ń Ó Ś Ź acutes (Ń unique; Ś/Ź otherwise only Montenegrin), Ż dot (unique); consonant clusters, no V but lots of **W** (only Slavic language), Z/K/C everywhere
Generally **flat & green**, agriculture + forests (mountains only near the southern border); many **tree-lined roads**
Neatly **tiled sidewalks** (grey/light red, varied patterns) — sometimes even tiled roads
Central-European: light/pastel stucco, orange/red/black/brown roofs w/ 'plastic-tidy' tiles; blocky detached homes; communist-era (often renovated) apartment blocks; dense historic downtowns
Very Catholic: roadside **Christian shrines** + blue-white/yellow-white Catholic flags
Rare Gen4 black or white car (both also elsewhere in Europe)
**3-digit regional road numbers cluster by first digit** (yellow boxes on signs; 2-digit roads don't follow this); **area codes**: first 2 digits regional — even the first digit alone narrows a lot
**Suffix meta**: -owo = north, **-ów = south** (think Kraków), -ino/-yno = northwest, -ew = centre
Rare **bilingual signs**: Kashubian (umlauts+accents) → north; German → Opole; Lithuanian → Puńsk; Belarusian → east border; Lemko (Cyrillic) → southeast
**South border = hilly/mountainous** (rest mostly flat); traditional **wooden houses** → east (Podlaskie); orchards → around Grójec & Sandomierz

**Pole markings**: yellow → west+south; black → southeast (+north pockets); white background → Podlaskie (occasionally Łódź); lamps **on top of the pole** → northeast (+far-south pockets), side-mounted lamps rare in the NE

**Gdańsk**: Dutch-Golden-Age downtown (Amsterdam-like); street signs: Białystok = turquoise w/ district; Szczecin = green-blue; Poznań = black w/ blue district section

hints.text: **White stone houses w/ orange tiled roofs**; blue/yellow painted lines around windows/doors/wall edges; decorative **mosaic-tile facades**
Gen4 smallcam coverage

**Faro** (south): decorative chimneys — round or square w/ spire tops (round = strongest clue); **north**: square concrete-block houses w/ **white mortar** (Galicia similar but w/o the mortar)

**Montado** (sparse holm/cork oaks) → southeast (dry in summer, lush in spring); olive rows → east (far NE + SE) — olives in dense rows vs oaks sparse & patternless; dense eucalyptus forests → northwest; maritime-pine forests → northern half (also Galicia); **terraced Douro vineyards** → east of Porto; citrus → south coast

**Serra da Estrela**: rocky highland + **yellow-black striped snow poles**; sandy-white pine forests → Setúbal↔Sines coast; far southwest = flat, dry, near-treeless bushland
**Lime-green street signs**
US overseas territory — **counts as US** in country streaks

hints.text: Predominantly flat **sand desert** — few trees or greenery

Single-family **pastel stone houses**; roofs orange tile → grey metal; walls of **painted square tiles** in patterns

**Dacia > 25% of all cars** (no other Eastern-European country >10%)

bullets: **Caucasian fir** (Caucasus only): straighter, well-organised branches

hints.text: **Flower metas**: fireweed (pink) → north; sosnowsky hogweed (white) → Moscow/Komi/Perm; sunflowers → Ukraine border + Ulyanovsk/Samara/N-Orenburg; blue-pod lupines → N-Vladimir/E-Ivanovo/SW-Kostroma
**Tree metas near borders**: black poplar → south (Ukraine border) + west Kazakh border; Russian olive → Kazakhstan border
**Sandy roadsides** → Khanty-Mansi, Yamalo-Nenets, around Nizhny Novgorod (Volga), Karelia, Murmansk, W-Sakha (beware: also near rivers elsewhere)
**Red soil** → around Izhevsk & Perm, much of Arkhangelsk, Leningrad & Pskov Oblast, near Volgograd/Astrakhan (non-exhaustive — appears near water/iron mines anywhere)
**Far-north tundra**: relatively flat with very low vegetation

**Caucasus** = one of Russia's largest ranges; Mt. **Elbrus** (highest) on the Kabardino-Balkaria / Karachay-Cherkessia border
**Rocks along the road** → most of Karelia & Murmansk (smaller/rarer in Perm Krai & Buryatia)
**RHD Japanese import cars** get commoner the further **east** you go (from ~Novosibirsk) — fast east/west tell
Very **thin stick-mounted bollards** → eastern half, starting around Omsk (normal bollards still plentiful)
**Black traffic-sign bottoms** → Stavropol, Chechnya, W-Dagestan (rare uncoloured exceptions in other southern subjects)
Roads made of **concrete blocks** → the north: Komi, Nenets, Yamalo-Nenets
Town names in **Cyrillic + Latin** → near the Baltic border & around Moscow (less often near Finland)
**Brown tourist signs** → Moscow Oblast + bordering oblasts (elsewhere only near attractions/city centres)
**Red-brick-only buildings** → mostly south (+ Magnitogorsk, Orsk, Omsk); more brick in view = more reliable
**Mosques** → Muslim-majority south + Tatarstan & Bashkortostan
**Full coverage map** — very sparse in the east & north; knowing the gaps helps
**Gen3 white car (long antenna)** hotspots: southern Chelyabinsk, snow coverage around Novosibirsk, Far East from Amur Oblast
bullets: Kamchatka's 'silver' car often looks white (B-type antenna) — the main exception to black/white

hints.text: **Short-antenna types** (Gen3) region-guess Russia
bullets: **A-type** (ridge right): Kaluga, Orenburg, Krasnodar
**B-type** (ridge left): Nizhny Novgorod, Elista, Yekaterinburg–Tyumen
**B pronounced** (rectangular ridge): Moscow Obl, Kurgan, Krasnoyarsk, SW-Krasnodar
**C-type** (ridges lower, 'twisting'): north & east of Moscow, down to Rostov
**Blurred**: two clusters — around Ufa and Stavropol→Makhachkala
**Tilted** (leans left): St. Petersburg → Volgograd diagonal (+Stavropol, Ufa)

hints.text: **Long-antenna types** (Gen3)
bullets: **A-type** (ridge right): Leningrad Obl, Karelia, Vologda — almost all WHITE-car long antenna is A-type
**B-type** (ridge left): Yaroslavl→Vladimir, Tula, much near Tuva & Irkutsk
**Blurred**: Sakhalin & eastern roads, much of Khanty-Mansi (+far-west Bryansk, some Tula)

hints.text: **Gen4 seasons** region-guess
bullets: **Winter**: south — Ryazan down to Kabardino-Balkaria & Dagestan; heavy Gen4 snow → Saratov + E-Voronezh
**Spring**: Kemerovo/Krasnoyarsk, Orenburg/Kamyshin, Lipetsk, Moscow city
**Summer**: the default nearly everywhere
**Autumn**: rare — Kazan, few Murmansk roads, some Ulan-Ude
Altai mountains & most of the Far East have **no Gen4** at all

hints.text: **Gen4 car colours**
bullets: **Red** (most common): Khanty-Mansi, highways down to Irkutsk/Ulan-Ude, Penza/Tatarstan, Rostov-on-Don
**White**: May coverage near Lipetsk, Moscow city, all coverage north of Berkakit (Sakha) + Magadan
**Black**: Moscow, Tver, Voronezh (+some Tatarstan & south)

hints.text: Area codes — the leading digit gives the zone:
bullets: **3-** = central
**4-** = east or around Moscow (401 Kaliningrad, ~410–430 far east, 470+ Moscow region; Moscow city = 495/499)

hints.text: Gen3 sometimes has **unblurred plates** — regional code on the right; ordered alphabetically within subject type (01 = Adygea, 28 = Amur; 3-digit: drop first digit, 123 → 23 Krasnodar)

**Chuvashia**: 'урамӗ'

**Komi**: 'улича'

**Blue tourist signs with English translations** — only in Moscow

**Red-painted poles** — only in Murom
**Angled extended insulator bar** on poles
Pedestrian signs on a **U-shaped hook**

bullets: **Vorkuta** (N-Komi): dead-looking winter, sometimes reddish soil, big apartment-block foundations

hints.text: **Baikal & south spotlights**

hints.text: Plates **long white front / yellow back**

Limited coverage (mainly **Kigali** + patches); street names carry a **2-letter district code**; national roads paint the road number on lampposts

hints.text: Houses of **wooden panels, boxy, in vivid colours**

hints.text: Well-kept colourful prefab blocks (like CZ); **diamond-tile roofs** (also HU); window shutters much commoner than CZ (also HU); houses w/ **short side to the road + hip-end roof** (also HU); grey metal roofs → east

hints.text: **Slovak: Ľ Ô Ä Ŕ Ĺ** (Czech: Ř Ě Ů); acute vowels in both; street = **ulica**

**Skalica** = Czech-like red street signs w/ stylised font; **Čičmany** = dark wooden houses covered in white line art

videos.title: Never Mix Up Australia and South Africa Again

hints.text: Drives on the **left**
**Single yellow outer + white centre lines** on most roads; occasionally unique **triple centre lines** (2 solid white + dashed inside, or white-yellow-white) on main roads

shared_with.note: Same yellow outers + triple centres in southern Africa.
shared_with.diff: N/R road numbers → ZA; A/B → Botswana.

shared_with.note: Australia practically NEVER uses yellow outer lines.
shared_with.diff: Yellow outers → southern Africa, not Australia.

hints.text: **Gen2 quite common** (big circular blur + sky halo) — the only African country with Gen2; Gen4 = **blue car w/ short antenna**; smallcam exists (short antenna on the back)
hints.uniq_note: African Gen2 round = South Africa, full stop.

hints.text: 11 official languages — **English** dominates signage; **Afrikaans** (Dutch-like): suffixes -fontein, -kraal, -kop, -dorp, -nek, -berg/-burg — found nationwide (NOT region-useful)
Most common pole = **'bird poles'**: 1–5 horizontal bars w/ thin white insulators like rows of perched birds
hints.uniq_note: The southern-African signature pole — dominant in ZA.

hints.text: **Green directional signs w/ yellow road numbers**; number format = country: **N & R = South Africa only** (A/B = BW/LS/NA; +C/D/M/F = Namibia; MR = Eswatini)
hints.uniq_note: The N/R prefix instantly separates ZA from its lookalike neighbours.

hints.text: Triangular **red-border warning signs** (Australia/NZ use yellow diamonds); chevrons **white-red** + unique thin single-arrow mini chevrons roadside
Suburban houses ringed by **high walls, fences & gates**
Each **province = own plate design**, some visible through blur: Free State = greenish tint, KwaZulu-Natal = bluish

**N-highway map** (maps show a blue pentagon instead of the N); **3-digit R-roads geographically grouped** (map; 100s scattered); **area codes**: 10 digits, 0 + 2 regional digits — even the first digit narrows it (06–08 = mobile, useless)
**Pole tops by province**:
bullets: 'A'-shape (3 bars, insulators on a horizontal bar): almost only Western Cape
Wider 'A', insulators directly on the A: Eastern Cape
Trident tops: almost unique to KwaZulu-Natal
3 alternating horizontal insulators: Limpopo/Gauteng/North West/Mpumalanga (+ some Northern Cape)
'<'-shaped diagonal bars: NE, esp. Limpopo & North West
1–2 metal arms holding insulators: Mpumalanga
Big bird NESTS on poles: northern Northern Cape

hints.text: **Sticker metas**: red squares on sign backs → Eastern Cape (+ nearby NC/FS/NW; on CHEVRON backs = everywhere); white stickers on chevron backs → the Cape provinces; grainy pinkish 'Texas' pavement → Limpopo; power plants → Secunda↔eMalahleni (Mpumalanga)
**Landscape**:
bullets: NE: dry orange-red soil, shrubby hills (seasonally green!)
Western Cape: rocky treeless mountains w/ steep sides + sharp peaks (Lesotho similar)
Vineyards: almost only Western Cape valleys (+ Upington, NC)
Northern Cape: dry red-soil desert
Sugarcane: KwaZulu-Natal coast (+ Mpumalanga/Mozambique border)
Tall Cook pines: East London↔Durban coast
Pine plantations: west of Eswatini + N-KwaZulu-Natal
Grassy rolling hills + ridges: coastal WC (Cape Town↔George)
Mozambique border area: flat + green subtropical

hints.text: **Kruger NP** (Mozambique border): camouflage car w/ black mirrors + roof racks (now also Gen4); flat dry savanna, red soil, no farms, distinctive **directional waystones** at junctions; gravel or lineless paved roads

**City metas**: unique M-route signs → Port Elizabeth (Gqeberha) & Durban; traffic lights wrapped in **3 reflective tape strips** → Johannesburg; Durban lamp arm w/ a bump on top; **dodgy abortion/enlargement ads** on poles → Gauteng (Joburg, eMalahleni)

**Spotlights**: R360 north of Bokspits = reddish asphalt, arid w/ grey trees + golden grass, N-S; **Table Mountain** (3-km plateau + pyramidal Devil's Peak) → west of Cape Town; George's Peak visible north of George
British Overseas Territory east of the Falklands (counts as UK); only a couple of **Gen3 trekkers** (Grytviken + 3 fjord trekkers), none on streets — memorise them
**Grytviken** = the only pinpointable trekker (appears on community maps) — abandoned rusted machinery, a church + white-painted wooden buildings
A wide open **valley w/ lightly snow-capped mountains** on the west side of South Georgia Island
**Prion Island** = wooden walkway, lush green plants & many **seals**; a boat trekker in Hercules Bay shows a man in a red jacket + the MS National Geographic Explorer
Plates: pre-2006 short **green**; post-2006 **white** private / **yellow** commercial (long & short)
Almost all road coverage = **Gen3**; car back usually visible — **black, white or silver**, never an antenna
Much coverage in **autumn/winter** (spring/summer not uncommon)
**Hangul** — very recognisable **circles in the letters**, unlike other East-Asian scripts
Utility poles: **black-yellow diagonal stripe markings** near the bottom + tapered **spike on top**

shared_with.diff: Taiwan: stripes reach **all the way down** and look more **orange**; Korea: stripes stop above the ground and are more yellow. Hangul vs Chinese characters settles it.

hints.text: Drives on the **right**
**Yellow middle + white outer lines**; sometimes **red block markings** on the road; speed bumps painted **yellow-white**
Warning triangles **yellow** (prohibition circles & yield = white); direction signs **green with white text**; street signs **blue arrow-shaped**, often hanging from a bar on the pole
Pedestrian-crossing signs: **pentagonal shape + blue background**
**House-shaped blue house-number plaques** with the full address in Hangul + Latin (regional variants exist → spotlight)
Black-white **wedge-shaped bollards** — round **yellow reflector front**, grey back
Predominantly **mountainous** — even flat areas show elevation on the horizon
**Red brick** very common; traditional **tiled roofs** w/ decorated corners, slightly upcurved sides — **blue roofs** frequent
**Coverage limited & spotty** — mostly big highways + major cities
**Area codes increase southward**: 02 Seoul (only 2-digit) → 064 Jeju
**Gen2** almost only Seoul & Busan; small **camera above the brake light** → NE of Seoul + around Pohang
Volcanic island: **dark volcanic dirt**, volcano views, **black rock walls**
One of the only **completely flat** areas — open yellow-green fields along nearly every road
House numbers with a **large white section on top**
Plates long white, single **blue strip left**

shared_with.note: Portuguese plates usually have a **yellow strip right**.

shared_with.diff: Two strips + short front plate → Italy.

hints.text: Standard bollard: **yellow-orange front reflector + two white dots on the back** (back can be blank), hollow

hints.text: **Ladder poles** + wooden poles in rural areas; 'French' poletops (upside-down triangle, curved top bar); **pylons far more common** than in other Mediterranean countries

shared_with.diff: Blue pole plates → France; Spanish → Spain.

hints.text: Road markers with **colourful borders** (code on top = road number, letters = region-specific!)
Rural **hunting signs**: white 'coto … caza' or diagonal b&w — both unique to Spain (code readable = region, e.g. Ex = Extremadura)
A-type guardrails with **yellow reflectors**
Pedestrian signs with **8 stripes** — Europe-max (only Andorra matches); stop signs with a distinctly **small font** (Portugal: large font)
hints.uniq_note: 8-stripe crossings exist only in Spain and Andorra — everything else in Europe has fewer stripes.

hints.text: Red sign borders **reach the edge** (FR/IT/PT keep a thin white edge); nearly all signs on **flat posts** (Europe: only PT/FR/AD too)
Chevrons: **black w/ white** AND **blue w/ white** (only 2 or 4 arrows; France has 1–5); direction signs white w/ **colourful road-number boxes**, highways blue
Rural roads sometimes have **dashed outer lines** (≈ nonexistent in PT/IT)

**Spanish**: ñ unique; vs Portuguese: PT uses ç/lh/-ão/nh + da/do contractions; street = **calle**; garage signs '**Vado permanente**' (+municipality after 'Ayuntamiento de'; Italy: 'Passo carrabile')
Highly diverse: lush Atlantic north → bone-dry semideserts; plateaus, rolling hills, jagged ranges; default = dry Mediterranean
Gen3: long/short/no antenna (back black or white when visible); Gen4: **blue** or black car; smallcam; some old **shitcam** exists

**Road-code map**: regional letter codes on markers (SG = Segovia …); national = E/AP/A/R/N (A also Andalusia/Aragón!); Asturias = many local codes; Melilla = ML; **area codes**: 2 digits after the 9 ≈ province

**Catalan** (Catalonia/Valencia/Balearics — dominant in CAT+BAL): 'i' = and, grave accents à è ò, ç; street = **carrer**; 'Gual permanent'; **Basque** (K/Z, street = kalea/karrika, bilingual black-border sub-signs — Basque on top in Basque Country, Spanish on top in Navarre); **Galician** (≈Portuguese but with ñ, no ç; street = **Rúa**; towns starting A/O/As/Os = Galicia-unique)
**Regional flags** of the autonomous communities appear often — learnable; Catalan-independence Senyera flags in Catalan areas

**Topography**: Pyrenees NE + Baetic/Sierra Nevada SE highest; Meseta Central plateau ringed by Cantabrian/Iberian/Sierra Morena; north coast = green & lush (UK/Ireland-like); Pyrenees = light grey rock + pines + rock walls (like Andorra/French side; Llívia exclave!); Sierra Nevada = only southern range snowy most of the year

**Region looks**: Castilla y León = flat cereal + sunflowers; La Rioja = vineyards between two ranges; Ebro strip (Navarra→Aragón) = semi-desert w/ sandy soil + shrubs; central/south Aragón = arid flats (white rocky north, red Teruel); Castilla-La Mancha = dead-flat + half of Spain's wine + olive rows (Ciudad Real/Toledo); Extremadura = **dehesa** (open oak fields — don't mistake for olives); Murcia/S-Alicante = arid, sandy, hazy rugged mountains; Málaga coast = white hillside villages

**Olives & oranges**: olives in half the country — Córdoba/Jaén = endless rolling-hill plantations (big ones Gen2 only); S of Madrid = small flat fields + red soil; east coast = fenced/rock-walled + mountains in view; oranges → east coast (small valleys) + Guadalquivir basin (open & flat)

**Trees**: eucalyptus → north coast (Galicia most; dry eucalyptus → Huelva); corn → river basins + north coast; stone pines → Valladolid/Huelva flats; Aleppo pines → east coast + Balearics; Baltic pines → Pyrenees/Guadarrama/Iberian System; maritime pines Atlantic vs Mediterranean variants (flat plantations N of Segovia)
**Roadline region map** (solid vs dashed outer on small roads); Araba = long-dash middles; Gipuzkoa = long outer dashes; Catalonia = atypical mixes

**Km-marker metas**: rectangular vs square = half-country split; Andalusia = extra-tall w/ governing body; Madrid = square + regional flag; Biscay = big w/ semicircle top; national roads = red rectangle; border colours orange/green/yellow by road size (some regions unique)

**Regional infra**: Galicia 'Concello' municipality strips (green/blue); Asturias = red CTA bus stops (Madrid's EMT similar), COGERSA bins, **yellow snow poles** (unique); Aragón = flat stick bollards; Catalonia = round corrugated posts (also Basque/Balearics; France-like), Catalan hunting signs, big square 1-arrow chevrons; Lleida/Tarragona = square-orange-reflector bollard (also E-Canaries); **Murcia = red-arrow square chevrons (exclusive)** + rectangular mesh poles; Madrid = round-top red bus stops + green buses; **Extremadura = green-top bollards (exclusive)**; Castilla-La Mancha = blue/white stone field markers; Andalusia = green-stripe Junta signs + **blue-yellow snow poles (unique)**

**Architecture**: slate roofs → NW (ends abruptly east; also Pyrenees near Andorra + NE of Madrid); Galicia = concrete-block houses + **hórreos gallegos** granaries; Asturias = square hórreos on 4 pillars (slate roof → west Asturias); Basque/Cantabria = big white houses w/ wooden frames; Catalonia/E-Aragón = stone **masías**; Andalusia/Extremadura = all-white towns
**Car metas**: Asturias = black car, no antenna, visible brake light (rare Galicia spill); Gen3 **white-top antenna** → E-Andalusia + Huesca/Lleida (like Gibraltar's)

**Balearics**: coastal Mediterranean, Aleppo-pine hills, fruit/olive patches, **low rock walls everywhere** (Bari/Italy similar); all-white houses w/ low-incline tiled roofs; grazing fields ≈ only Menorca; Formentera = low shrubs + Ibiza mountains visible; NW-Mallorca = the only big Balearic range

**Canary Islands**: extremely diverse (volcanic, arid, pine forests) — 'Spain but the vibe is off' → think Canaries; Lanzarote = moon-like dark volcanic soil + **semicircle rock vineyards**; Fuerteventura = arid + orange soil (archipelago-unique); Las Palmas province = square-orange-reflector bollards (Tenerife province: standard, highways only); **La Graciosa** = barren, overcast, white flat-roof towns

**Ceuta & Melilla** (N-African enclaves): flat-roof warm-coloured houses; Ceuta = sunny saturated Gen4, eucalyptus+pine mountains, **b&w guardrails (Spain-unique)**; Melilla = hazy, cloudy, little rural coverage
**Camino de Santiago**: yellow scallop on blue on signs/waystones (all over Europe but denser near Santiago) + pilgrims in summer

**Spotlights**: N-Castilla-La-Mancha = early-spring dirt roads, green fields + leafless vineyards on red soil (Gen3; similar Gen4 further south); AP-15 (Navarra) = flat bollards w/ square red reflector + red guardrail reflectors; **Ebro delta** = dead-flat rice + canals, mountains S/W/N; El Ejido (Almería↔Motril) = white-roof greenhouse sea; Doñana NP = rare unique car
**Barcelona**: black taxis w/ yellow doors; **Bilbao**: uniquely ornate street-sign frames (nowhere else in Spain)
Plates: **long white front / short yellow rear**
Gen3: car front shows **blue-white-red patches** through the blur ('French flag', red most visible)
hints.uniq_note: This car is unique to Sri Lanka — Ghana's coverage shows a similar car front, but that car also carries a roof rack.

hints.text: All **Gen4 = low-cam** (bigger blur, wide-looking roads, low angle) — Gen3 here is normal height!

shared_with.note: All Swiss coverage is low-cam (car blurred).

hints.text: **Sinhala** (very curvy, rounded) everywhere + **Tamil** (straighter) regionally; some English
hints.uniq_note: Sinhala is unique to Sri Lanka — but Tamil is also found in India's Tamil Nadu; a Tamil-only sign alone doesn't settle the country.

hints.text: Concrete poles: **square w/ pinholes only in the top part** + **holey poles** (big see-through holes, lower half)

shared_with.note: Thai square poles look similar — but the pinholes run **all the way down**.
shared_with.diff: Pinholes bottom-to-top → Thailand; top-only → Sri Lanka.

shared_with.note: Vietnam has holey poles too — with much **smaller** holes.

hints.text: Short, wide tapered **stone bollards — black base, white top**
Tall white concrete **waystones with an indented bottom section + yellow road number**
**Black-white striped signposts**
Rural roads: **outer lines but no middle line** (most small roads have none at all) — good 50-50 decider
Tropical: lush green, densely populated, agricultural patchwork; often **reddish soil** (sometimes white sand); **palm trees** everywhere; orange/red **tiled roofs** with brownish wear
70% **Buddhist**: temples, Buddha statues, stupas, Buddhist flags everywhere (minorities: Hindu 13%, Muslim 10%, Christian 7% — strongly regional)
**Tuktuks**: bulky shape w/ black roof — green/yellow/red common; some low-quality **'shitcam'** coverage exists (wide circular blur, brownish)
**Area codes**: landlines = 0 + 2 mapped digits (mobiles 07x non-regional)
**Religion = region**: Buddhism most of the country (absent north/parts of east coast); Hinduism north + east + Nuwara Eliya; Islam strongest east coast; Christianity northwest (Mannar)
**Tamil script ≈ Hindu areas**: native Tamils north+east; Indian Tamils around Nuwara Eliya; Tamil-ONLY signage → Tamil-majority areas (bilingual signs appear everywhere)

**Topography**: hills/mountains concentrated in the central-south; southwest = wet climate (deep green, dense); rest = drier (lower, open, brown-green mix — never truly arid; Gen3 looks more arid than reality)
**Tea plantations** → central + southwest, most just west of **Nuwara Eliya** (compact bright-green shrubs in rows on hillsides)
Distinct hilly **long-grass plains**, forests only in the distance
Arctic archipelago under Norwegian sovereignty — **counts as Norway** in country streaks
Plates generally **black w/ yellow text**
hints.uniq_note: Mainland Norway uses white plates — black/yellow in an Arctic setting → Svalbard.

hints.text: **Barren, treeless mountains**; vegetation = only moss & grasses; sky often overcast, gloomy coverage
hints.uniq_note: Iceland is the lookalike — Svalbard is even more barren, with zero trees and Arctic light.

hints.text: **Russian** sometimes on signs (Soviet-era mining settlements)
**Longyearbyen**: largest settlement, many **boxy minimalistic buildings**
**Pyramiden**: feels abandoned — Soviet-style multi-storey blocks, no human activity, **pyramidal mountains** in the distance
**Barentsburg**: most desolate of the three towns; **multi-coloured apartment blocks** near the hilltop; even gloomier coverage

**Road spotlights**: SE of Longyearbyen = treeless mountains both sides + lakes to the mountains (small black mirror sometimes left); south of the airport = trekker holding a **red car** on a winding road (or the distinct NE mountain line)
**Boat coverage**:
bullets: Large ship → NW Spitsbergen NP (coverage gets darker toward the east)
Wooden-deck boat → several places
Small red boat w/ men in red jackets → northwest
Black-deck boat → Isfjorden (Barentsburg–Grumant)
Slanted-wheelhouse boat → east of Pyramiden

videos.title: This is how you Guess Sweden 97,4% of times!

hints.text: Plates long white + EU strip; **taxis = yellow plates**
**Swedish: Å Ä Ö**

shared_with.note: Danish has Å but æ/ø instead of ä/ö.

shared_with.diff: æ ø + yellow middle lines → Norway.

shared_with.note: Icelandic has Ö but no Å/Ä (þ ð instead).
Finnish has Ä Ö (Å in Swedish text); words much longer w/ double vowels.

hints.text: **All lines white**: small roads = short dashed outer lines (shorter than Norway's); highways = sectioned 'solid' outer line pattern (Denmark's sections are rectangular)

shared_with.note: Finland also uses yellow (older) / white (newer) lines.
Similar sectioned highway lines — but rectangular sections.

hints.text: Bollards black-white w/ **grey reflector** (orange at intersections); wedge, round or thin-curved
Snow poles **orange w/ tall white reflector slightly above the middle** (sometimes a thinner second one above)

shared_with.note: Finnish reflectors are much thinner & near the top.
Norwegian poles: 1–2 thinner reflectors, one near the middle.

hints.text: Wooden poles w/ small **black metal cap on top** (Nordic-only); lamps mounted slightly **below the top w/ double bolting** (Finland: top-mounted, straighter); wooden **electricity markers w/ blue+white stripe** = Sweden-only in the Nordics
hints.uniq_note: The blue-white electricity markers exist in no other Nordic country; the pole cap is pan-Nordic.

hints.text: Pedestrian signs **4 stripes** (+female-silhouette version!); FI/DK = 5 stripes, NO = 4 or 5

Directional signs **blue w/ thin white arrow** or **yellow w/ red border** (tiny places); road signs = **yellow background + red border** (Finland adds a thin yellow outer border); 'M' meeting-place signs (Norway's M font differs)

shared_with.note: Yellow signs too — with an extra thin yellow border outside the red.
Directional signs yellow/white; M-sign font differs (middle point reaches the bottom).

hints.text: Chevrons **yellow arrows on blue** — only country in Europe (very rare in Luxembourg); much infra painted **red+yellow** (roadwork signs, blocks, even chevrons); signpost stripes: blue-white (also FI/EE) but **red-yellow/blue-red/white-red ≈ Sweden-only**
hints.uniq_note: Yellow-on-blue chevrons: only Sweden (Luxembourg has ultra-rare ones). The red-yellow infra scheme is the fastest Sweden tell.

hints.text: Street suffixes **-vägen / -gatan** (short v/g; gränd near centres); Norway: veien/gate attached; Denmark: vej/gade
Countryside: wooden houses in **Falu-red** paint (only SE/NO/FI); urban = modern concrete/brick
Boreal forests, farms, many lakes; N+W mountainous w/ narrow lakes, S+E flatter; traditional **diagonal-stacked wooden fences** (rare in other Nordics)
**Volvo ≈ 30%** of cars (NO/FI ~10%); Swedish **church flag** (red cross on yellow + crown) near churches
Gen3: white car w/ **long antenna** (all Iceland coverage has this too; some Finland); Gen4: **red, white or blue** car (only the blue exists in NO/FI/DK); smallcam

shared_with.note: All Icelandic road coverage = white long-antenna car.

hints.text: **Vegetation zones**: broadleaf (non-birch) → southern half; ferns → south+centre; treeless mountain tops → NW only; thin weak boreal trees + sandy soil → far north; beech forests → far south; **rock walls** → south + Öland + Gotland tips (church walls everywhere)

**Landscape regions**: west-coast round exposed rocks w/ bushes (Stockholm's are sharper + mossy); Skåne = most open farmland, diverse crops; Mälaren = flat fields w/ tree 'islands'; Väster-/Östergötland = open + **wind turbines**; Halland = flat fields + round forested hills; Umeå–Luleå coast = flat small fields; Jämtland = grass farmland on wide steep hills; High Coast = rocky forest mountains + curvy valleys; Södermanland = fields through small valleys

**Islands**: Gotland = white roadside sand (north + Fårö), wedge-cut fences, limestone houses w/ dirty-white facades, distinct churches; **Öland** = dead-flat, barren bushy south / lusher north, many small rock walls + uneven stone brick, most **windmills** in Sweden; red dirt roads → Dalarna (+Kalmar, Norrbotten/Västerbotten border); grey dirt roads w/ green edges → N-Jämtland; birch rows in northern coastal towns

**Architecture regions**: grey metal roofs → north; Skåne = white-stone/red-brick w/ coloured wooden gable triangles, half-timbered (south Skåne + S-Halland), older red brick (like Denmark), mossy corrugated barn roofs, grey diamond-tiled roofs (+Blekinge/Öland); west coast = light/white-yellow houses (Gothenburg→Norway) + dense red fishing sheds; **Dalarna** = red timber + log portals + härbren; Hälsingland = grand many-windowed red farmhouses; north coast = octagonal/outward-leaning log sheds

**Bus-stop signs & shelters are region-specific** (big learnable infographics; cities have own local variants; neon-yellow school signs → Linköping/Norrköping/Jönköping; Halland stripes on Västra-Götaland stops = Kungsbacka; black shelters w/ square roof ridge = Västerås + Umeå)

**Road numbers**: national 9–99, regional 100–404, increasing to the **northeast**; railway-crossing sign backs: yellow 5-digit sticker, **first digit = region** (NE-increasing; 6 = random); **area codes**: first 2 digits regional (Stockholm = 08), 3rd/4th increase NE; snowmobile-trail red crosses → northern half
**Toponym-ending map** (incl. farmsteads); **Sámi** bilingual signs → inland north; **Meänkieli** (Finnish-like, rivers ending 'väylä') → Finnish border (trilingual w/ Sámi possible)

**Bollard regions**: German-style wedges → south; cylindrical w/ horizontal black section → west coast + Värmland/S-Dalarna; flat (or black-rectangle) → northern major roads; small white w/ blue or red strip → rural Skåne; green snow poles → around Umeå; red roads + thin pines → W-Dalarna
White Google car w/ **blue stripe on the back** → west coast + Skåne (almost never outside Sweden)

**North spotlights**: E10 Kiruna→Norway = small birches + snowy mountains + big lake north; road 99 north = grey sandy ground + ditch + low mixed forest; Ritsem road = big lake south + dramatic mountains; the only paved road **above the treeline** = Jämtland/Västerbotten border; highest public (dirt) road = Flatruet (cloudy Gen4 summer); **Kiruna** = staircase-shaped mine mountains; Gällivare = smooth treeless mountain SW

**South spotlights**: Omberg = lone forested ridge west of flat fields; mistletoe → Mälaren/Västerås; Visingsö island in Vättern; orchards → Kivik (+Vånga, Båstad, SE-Vättern); N-Öland coastal road = rocky beach + low bushes; Fårö = dry stony fields w/ junipers; Hoburgen = south-Gotland west-coast road w/ grassy ridge east

**Architecture spotlights**: Walloon-mill factory towns N/E of Uppsala (identical row houses + belfries); blue/green **slag-stone buildings** → Bergslagen; crown-topped brick chimneys → Lake Siljan; **Visby** = Hanseatic cobblestone + bright stone houses + roses + town wall; Gamla Stan Stockholm = warm-coloured old stone over narrow alleys

**Street signs**: Stockholm = two rows split by a black line (suburbs plain white; also rare central Gävle); Gothenburg = unique blue two-liners; Malmö = old white w/ black corners; Ystad = same but yellow; Vellinge = Gothenburg-like but one line; Falköping = plain yellow; Köping = blue w/ thin black outline; 'gattu/gatu' endings → central Dalarna; **Elfdalian** ('-ween/-weg') → Älvdalen only; trams → Gothenburg (+Norrköping, Stockholm, Gen4 Lund)

**Misc spotlights**: 3 yellow stripes on posts → Karlstad (+Stockholm traffic lights); tree-felling ads on poles → Stockholm suburbs; dragon statues → Sundsvall ('Drakstaden'); E4 Umeå→Finland = divided w/ wide ditch, thin birch-pine forest; snow-pole combos near Norway: two wide reflectors close = E12, yellow poles w/ 2 reflectors = road 95, orange+yellow mix = E10

**Coverage metas**: road 95 W of Arjeplog = Gen4 thawing snow; Umeå = spring Gen4 w/ dirty snow piles (unique among cities); E45 Orsa→Älvros = wet brown-red pavement; Holmön = Gen3 white-antenna car w/ **visual glitch**; Foskrosvägen = red dirt + water smudges (find via 'Reindeer spot point')
ALL car coverage = **low-cam** (bigger blur, roads look wide, low angle)

hints.text: Plates **fully white, NO blue strip**; front plate extremely short; rear long or tall w/ **red Swiss emblem left + canton emblem right**
Bollards black-white w/ white/grey reflectors — wedge or cylindrical w/ rounded top (Liechtenstein nearly identical)

hints.text: Directional signs: **blue = main road, white = side road**; road signs often in a **round grey metal border**; town signs white/blue w/ metal frame (back: distances + canton abbreviation)

hints.text: Chevrons **black w/ white arrow** (rarely inverted; Austria = red-white/red-yellow)

**Architecture regions**: Ticino = Italian style (smooth orange-yellow stone, IT shutters); Bern area = thick arch-shaped roofs; northeast = elaborate **red timber frames**; Appenzell = house+barn at 90° w/ square-grid facades; Graubünden = all-stone houses w/ white painted window decor

**Canton infra**: Vaud = large town signs; Basel-Landschaft = **olive-green** posts/borders/backs; Valais = thick wooden bollards; Glarus = reflectors on fences instead of bollards; Geneva (+Martigny valley) = simplified bike symbol w/ disconnected wheels; road-divider types mapped

**Sankt Gallen** = blue street signs w/ rounded side edges (unique); **Basel** = big blue signs on low double posts (Denmark-like); **Bern** downtown = grey 4-storey buildings w/ rounded arcade openings
**Traditional Chinese** — one of the most complex scripts on Street View, many-stroke characters

hints.text: Drives on the **right** (Japan & Hong Kong drive left)
**Double yellow middle + single white outer lines** (thin roads: single dashed yellow); roads with **only outer white lines, no middle** are a great Taiwan tell
**Red outer lines** (no-parking), esp. in cities

hints.text: Chevrons **black on yellow**; intersection **mirrors with orange hoods**
Bollards: **2–3 circular reflectors on a black rectangle**; lollipop guardrail reflectors (Japan-like)
**Concrete drainage ditches** along roads; boxy **distribution transformers** (dark green/grey) esp. urban
Architecture: cramped multi-storey to flat single-storey; very **boxy**; walls covered in (usually white) **tiles**; light-green **tin sheds**
Gen3: **black car w/ long antenna + central red brake light** (Russia-like) or a car with a **huge blur** that looks like low-cam (but camera height is normal)
**Counties + outlying islands**: Kinmen & Lienchiang/Matsu (at the Chinese coast!), Penghu (mid-strait), Green & Orchid Island (SE, Taitung county) — beginners get caught out

**Area codes 02–08, increasing southward** (mobiles 09…); National Freeways: **odd = N-S, even = E-W**, numbers grow W→E & N→S (1 & 3 = long west-coast routes); township-road signs carry the **county character**
Blue **pole plaques = coordinates**: letter = 50×80 km grid cell, 4 digits = % east / % north within it (e.g. E3264) — precise region-guessing tool

**Landscape**: flat/rolling crop plains → central west; forested mountains → central-east two-thirds (high up: temperate/coniferous); **Hualien-Taitung valley** → flat plains between forested ranges E+W; **Yilan plain** → mountains N/W/S but open to the east, rice paddies w/ houses beside fields
Low rolling hills, winding roads, sparse **messy/unkempt farmland**; **Taichung**: open plains ringed by distant low mountains

**Vegetation metas**: areca/betel palms → central & south (mountains); coconut palms → southern coast; fish farms → west coast south half (Yunlin/Chiayi/Tainan/Kaohsiung); tea → mountains (Chiayi, Nantou, Hsinchu, Miaoli); Pingtung = areca in **neat flat-plain rows**
**Norfolk pines in tight rows** beside roads + many leafless trees; cream-white houses; **blue outer lines** almost exclusive here
Fuzzy-leaved trees lining roads + **red-tinged soil**; no Gen4; small traditional houses; lampposts w/ red-white diagonal stripes
**North**: old disorganised housing + dingy apartment blocks; **Taoyuan**: red-brick/red-tile apartments (also greater Taipei); **Yilan**: rich gated multi-storey villas

**Infra metas**: white-speckled pavement → east coast; Kaohsiung lampposts red-white stripes on **trapezoidal base**; Yunlin poles w/ **thicker support** below top; Changhua traffic boxes w/ **big yellow stickers**; Chiayi City **green square stickers** on every pole; bent chevron holders w/ 2 striped stickers → only Pingtung/Kaohsiung/Taitung; transformers on **dark-green raised holders** → New Taipei + Keelung
Tropical, coconut palms, lush cliffs, **run-down concrete pavement** — Green Island uses **yellow outer lines** and is more built-up (Orchid doesn't)
Hilly + frequent **concrete roads**, overcast coverage

**Road spotlights**: PH-8 (Taichung–Hualien) — **yellow guardrail** in W-Hualien, towering rock cliffs in the east; PH-20 through Yushan NP — conifer mountains + **tube-like double guardrails** (only Gen2 & overexposed sunny Gen4); Taipingshan road (Yilan) — fog, conifers, guardrail w/ **red legs** (some Gen3 sunset); Guangfu/Fenglin (Hualien) — short-tree forests around narrow grassy roads, often **Gen2**
**Rare finds**: green train car → east Chiayi near Alishan; blue pickup → Taipei riverside parks
Extremely limited coverage: **Gen3 trekkers in Gombe NP**, tripods on **Kilimanjaro**, + car spills on the Kenya border (none on roads / pinpointable)
**Kilimanjaro tripods**: high-altitude dark grey rock, eternal snow, mountaineers/tents; ethereal motion-blur look
**Gombe NP trekker**: lowland dense dark jungle (chimps) → savannah trees → bare grassland w/ Lake Tanganyika views
**Boat trekker** on Lake Tanganyika (olive-green & white boat; the coast is always to your east) — don't confuse with Madagascar's boats
One more tripod inside **Jane Goodall's house** (Gombe NP)
Plates large & **white** (passenger) / **yellow** (commercial)

hints.text: Street-light poles with **red-black or black-white striped bottoms**; distinct **long thin lamp posts** hanging over the road

hints.text: **Area codes**: Bangkok = 02, others 3 digits, geographically grouped — first digit already helps (mobiles 08/09, 06 non-regional)
**National road first digit** → region: 1 = north, 2 = northeast, 3 = centre, 4 = south (1–2 digit highways can cross regions)
Provincial roads: **2-letter Thai province abbreviation** on waystones & signs (never on national-road ones); black boxes carry **3-letter abbreviations** (ABC1234 or TCC-L2-ABC format)
**Far south = Muslim-majority**: Islamic attire, Jawi (Arabic-based) script, mosques

**Landscape metas**: flat & dry → north of Bangkok + east (east feels drier); tall mountains → northwest (+parts of peninsula); low smooth forested → centre-north; steep exposed rock → west + peninsula; red soil → mostly northeast; gravel roads → mapped areas

**Plantation metas (south)**: rubber (black taps on trunks) mostly south; >90% of palm oil in the south; areca palms on the peninsula (esp. Ranong); garden croton (yellow/orange/red leaves) around Ranong & coasts
**Rice**: main belt mapped; dry-season rice → central plains (dry season = wildfire haze in the sky); wet-season rice → eastern plains
**Coconut/palm metas**: dense short-coconut plantations → west of Bangkok; tall-coconut plantations → Prachuap Khiri Khan; masses of palmyra palms → east peninsula coast (S of Bangkok + far south)

**Crop metas**: sugarcane → central/east; cassava (digitate leaves) → central + NE plains; corn → north/central; fruit → Chiang Mai–Lamphun valley + E-Sa-Kaeo/Chanthaburi; durian → Chanthaburi; pineapple → SE & SW of Bangkok; eucalyptus plantations → Sa Kaeo; teak (big leaves, yellow flowers) → north; Tenasserim pines → north/northwest only
**Grey corrugated metal roofs** → mostly north & south

**Pole metas**: Chilean-style indented poles → around Bangkok (thicker versions on highways everywhere); insulators with **7+ ridges** → southern peninsula + Chiang Rai/Phayao highways (5–6 = standard); **1 insulator** on crossbar → peninsula south of Ranong/Chumphon, **2** → rest of country
**Wind turbines** → W-Chaiyaphum & W-Nakhon-Ratchasima + coast of Songkhla/Nakhon Si Thammarat
Black bollard with **three large white reflectors**
**Phuket**: touristy & wealthy — modern buildings, resorts, heavy traffic among hills; **Koh Samui & Koh Pha-Ngan**: narrow crowded tropical roads
Taxis **pink** and **yellow-on-green**; street signs with small green section under blue (Bangkok & Nonthaburi)
