hints.text: Road lines inconsistent: white, yellow, or mixed; **salt-and-pepper pavement** common; **divided highways extremely common** for a Mediterranean country
Chevrons: red-on-white (tip doesn't touch the edge, sometimes neon-yellow border); intersections: **black-yellow multi-arrow chevrons pointing up**; narrow red-white variant (≈ South Africa's)
Direction signs: distinct font with **noticeably wide letter spacing**; snow poles black-orange-black-white; stop sign says **DUR**
**Both A-type AND B-type guardrails** — seeing both excludes all of southern Europe/Middle East except Montenegro and Israel & the West Bank
Metal poles: **ladder-shaped or mesh pattern** — extremely common
Gen3: **never an antenna**; Gen4: white car (sliver of the back, short/no antenna) + **grey pickup truck** in some areas + **smallcam** (low mount, big blur w/ front protrusion)

shared_with.note: Similar grey pickup exists in Kenya (also Georgia, Senegal, Rwanda, Ghana, Paraguay).
shared_with.diff: African savanna vs Anatolian steppe; Turkish signage decides.

shared_with.note: Similar pickup (also in Georgia and Paraguay — no separate pages here).

hints.text: Landscape: arid SE → Mediterranean SW → lush forested north; most typical = **semi-arid rolling/flat vast farmland**
Tall square **apartment blocks** everywhere; Muslim-majority — **tall minarets** in most towns; distinctive **brick pavement** in cities
**Area codes**: first digit 2 (west) → 4 (east) (5xx not regional)

**Road numbering**: O-roads (O-xx on markers); D-roads (3-digit number + section number on top — sections increase to the SE); provincial roads: **first 2 digits = province code** (alphabetical 01–76)
**Gen4 truck coverage** → far east + south coast (+pockets west & around Kastamonu)
**North** = lusher & forested; **northeast** = treeless grassland valleys w/ round hills (deep green in Gen3, arid-looking in Gen4)

**Vegetation metas**: Turkish pines + olives → Mediterranean coast; tea → Rize; sunflowers → northwest; vineyards → east of Izmir; cotton → around Şanlıurfa; bananas → around Alanya; greenhouses → south coast west of Adana
**Lake Van**: biggest lake, semi-arid dry grassy hills; dark **volcanic rocks** → Şanlıurfa↔Diyarbakır; barren rocky river valleys → Erzurum↔Artvin + near Hakkâri
**Roof metas**: tiled → western ⅔; flat → southeast + south coast; metal → east; low-pyramid metal → around Lake Van; Nevşehir = big uneven sandstone bricks
**Chevron regions**: yellow-on-black → Balıkesir + Kars; black-on-yellow → north of Izmir; 4-lane divided w/ only double middle lines → eastern half

**Water-tank metas**: vertical tanks = most of Turkey (rare on S/W coast); horizontal-on-vertical → south coast line up to Elâzığ; square w/ angled corners → İskenderun↔Şanlıurfa; white spheres → SE, south of Batman
**European Turkey** (except Istanbul): 6-digit code plate under the yellow electricity warning on poles
Ankara: blue street signs w/ **small round top** (rarely Giresun; newer ones red); Izmir: dark blue, **bulging outward**, usually w/ white Izmir emblem
Red street signs w/ coloured bottom strip: **double-headed arrow with single arrowhead** → almost certainly Istanbul (+ red house numbers)
Trabzon (+Yalvaç): red **oval** street signs & house numbers; Bursa: green signs w/ extruded letters (green fences/guardrails too); Bolu: green but flat, borderless

**Spotlights**: Bodrum = whitewashed flat-roof buildings w/ blue details (Greek look); **Göreme** = sandstone monoliths w/ carved dwellings; **Mount Ararat** (Ağrı Dağı) = tallest peak + smaller Küçük Ağrı SE; Bozcaada island = light-blue/grey tourist signs

**Road spotlights**: E99 S of Şanlıurfa = hazy Gen4 sunrise, flat farmland, N-S angle; D525 S of Söke = dead-straight SSW-NNE winter Gen3 + lots of reed; W shores of Lake Beyşehir & Eğirdir = single-lane road, jagged rock + pines west (Eğirdir's road has a middle line)
Very limited coverage: **Kampala + Entebbe + national parks**; drives on the **left**
Plates **white front / yellow rear** (long or tall)
**Kampala** Google car: white, square side mirrors, boxy — unique to Kampala & its outskirts
Kampala = busy, narrow unpaved roads, small 1-storey houses w/ walls + barbed wire; **red soil** even on paved roads; very **hilly**
Kampala **taxi buses w/ a blue blocky pattern**; city-centre = black-and-white curbs + multi-storey buildings
**Entebbe** (SW of Kampala): emptier, greener, fancier houses, noticeably more street signs
National-park car = like Kampala's but with a **black bar along the front** (often not visible)
**Kidepo NP** (NE) = savannah w/ mountains all around (a wide mountain to the north)
Parks differ: **Murchison Falls** (low dirt road-barriers, palms north), **Lake Mburo** (driest, dried grass), **Kibale/Semuliki** (dense humid forest paths), **Queen Elizabeth** (non-red dirt)
Plates white + **blue-yellow strip left** (flag colours; old plates: strip hard to see)

shared_with.note: Russian plates fully white, NO strip.

hints.text: All coverage **Gen3**, mostly a **RED Google car** (± antenna; colour sometimes visible via blur glitch)
hints.uniq_note: The red Gen3 car is almost unique to Ukraine (parts of Belgium have a red long-antenna car). Vs Russia: no red car there, Russia has Gen4/Gen2 (Ukraine never), Russian Gen3 always has an antenna — antenna-less = think Ukraine.

hints.text: Rarer cars: black long-antenna, white long-antenna, grey short-antenna (regional — see below)
Some of **Europe's worst roads** — potholes, cracks, warped pavement (Russia's are generally better)
Random roadside objects (esp. fences) painted **blue-and-yellow**
**Ukrainian Cyrillic**: unique letters **Є Ґ І Ї** (vs Russian); street = **вулиця (вул.)**, city = **місто (м.)** (Russian: улица/ул., город/г.)

shared_with.note: Russian Cyrillic base looks the same.
shared_with.diff: **і/ї/є** or вул. → Ukraine; Ы/Э + ул. → Russia.

shared_with.note: Belarusian Cyrillic also uses і — plus **ў**.

hints.text: Square concrete poles (varied tops, white-painted bottoms) + **diagonal support beams**; wooden poles w/ concrete base blocks — post-Soviet kit (RU/LT similar)

shared_with.diff: Diagonal beams commoner in UA; plates/language decide.

hints.text: Chevrons **red w/ white arrows** (same in RU/EE); pedestrian signs **3 stripes** (also RU/EE/LT); white town-entry signs; **B&W-painted guardrails** (like Russia)
Soviet apartment blocks of **light-grey brick, rundown** (Baltic versions better kept); suburbs = fenced brick single-family houses, heavy vegetation, unpaved roads, corrugated roofs

**Rural vibe**: bad road + flat/slightly hilly + tree line then open fields + no infrastructure + no traffic; almost all coverage = **summer**; tall **poplars** common (most of Russia lacks them); ultra-rural tracks near cities exist despite the urban+main-road coverage rule
**Coverage map**: mostly cities + yellow roads; almost nothing in the SE (except Donetsk) and south of Odesa near Romania
**Area codes** per oblast (3rd digit 2 = oblast capital); **plate letter codes = oblast** (non-intuitive, learnable); oblast **second-level domains** (.te.ua = Ternopil) mostly intuitive

**May coverage** (yellowish leaf tint) → western half; **2011 autumn coverage** in 5 cities: Kyiv/Lviv/Kharkiv (mostly overwritten, white car), **Odesa** (mostly intact, silver short-antenna car), **Donetsk** (ONLY 2011, black long-antenna car — red car = never Donetsk)

**Carpathians** (west): hilly-mountainous, forested, pines, curvy roads; **Baltic-pine forests** → north near Belarus (flat!); **south** = warmer, drier, shrubs + open fields; blooming **sunflowers** → far south + east (Kherson/Mykolaiv/Odesa; T0403 to Nova Kakhovka)
**Russian language** → east + south (Ы/Э, ул., г.)

**Car metas**: leaf on the car's back → Ternopil↔Zolochiv/Brody/Dubno roads (H02/P39/E40/P26/E85); visual glitch → only Ivano-Frankivsk; yellow **Opel flag on the antenna** → one side of the Chernobyl road
**Red soil** on mine tracks + Brazil-style poles (bait! foliage isn't tropical + long antenna); poles w/ **red band** unique to Kryvyi Rih
**Sunflower bus shelters** (yellow-green, 'Оліс' brand) → Mykolaiv + Kherson oblasts; **'Polish' bollards** (wrap-around red reflector) → Zakarpattia
**Hungarian border area**: bilingual/monolingual Hungarian signs + Hungarian-style linear villages (single-storey, orange or diamond-slate roofs)
Special car trekker Ivankiv → **Pripyat**: inside Pripyat = abandoned, overgrown, eerie buildings
Typically a flat **desert** landscape
**Yellow outer lines**, middle white or yellow

shared_with.diff: No English on signs + black Google car → Jordan.
Oman's pickup antenna/pole details + generally more mountainous SE.

hints.text: Gen3: **white car with black roof racks**
hints.uniq_note: The only Middle-Eastern country with a white Google car — white cars exist on other continents, so pair with Arabic+English signage.

hints.text: Gen4: **white pickup w/ trekker camera** (often fully blurred) or black car w/ visible brake light

shared_with.diff: UAE: front antenna in the **middle** of the cabin; Qatar: on the **left**.
Check the antenna + Oman's mountains/kerb styles.

hints.text: **Arabic + English** on most signs (tourism)

hints.text: Residential houses of **light-coloured stone**; **reddish sidewalk stones**
**Black-white striped signposts** (in the Middle East also Oman); nearly all kerbs striped (b&w or black-yellow)

shared_with.diff: Oman: brown wooden poles similar; more mountainous; different plate shape.

hints.text: **Divided roads + roundabouts** everywhere; residential roads often **unpaved** despite the wealth
Poles: **dark-brown wood, 3 insulators symmetric on a horizontal bar**

shared_with.note: Jordanian lookalikes are **grey concrete** with wider-spaced insulators.

hints.text: **Camera rifts** occasionally (most common in Albania, Montenegro, Senegal)
Coverage mostly **northeast** (only exception with car coverage: Sir Baniyas Island; other remote spots = trekkers)
**Dubai skyline** w/ Burj Khalifa; mountains ONLY in the far east; N-Ras-al-Khaimah = light mountains east; mountains west + flat east = Gulf of Oman coast
Rare **white car w/ visible side mirrors** + sparse vegetation
Gen4 **sidewalk trekker** (sometimes black hair visible) incl. old town; **blue street signs w/ narrow metal border** specific to Dubai
**Trekker spotlights**: Al Ain (2 forts + zoo, far from car coverage); racetrack east of Abu Dhabi; remote dune trekkers in the far south (Taraq↔Alyhyali)
Plates **white front / yellow back**, usually WITHOUT blue strip (some have it; green strip = zero emissions)

shared_with.note: Irish plates are white front AND back, ALWAYS with a blue strip.
shared_with.diff: Yellow rear → UK; white rear + strip → Ireland.

hints.text: Drives on the **left** (Europe: only UK, Ireland, Malta, Cyprus)
**Double yellow lines** at road sides (no stopping — also Ireland); middle dashes w/ **cat's eyes** reflectors in the gaps
Standard bollard: rounded, **red front / white back reflector**, black base+top w/ white middle stripe; **yellow traffic bollards** common
Wooden poles w/ **metal pole steps** (thin horizontal rods); occasional crossbar w/ 2 diagonal supports (triangle)

shared_with.diff: Check the pole warning stickers: UK = man being zapped; Ireland = bolt only + extra outlined sticker.

hints.text: Chevrons **black w/ white arrows** (Ireland: yellow arrows; France: blue/white); triangular warning signs (Ireland = yellow diamonds!); yield = **GIVE WAY** (Ireland: YIELD)

shared_with.diff: Yellow chevron arrows, diamond warnings, YIELD, bilingual italic Irish signs → Ireland.

hints.text: Direction signs blue/green/white w/ **A- and M-road numbers** (B = smaller); street signs white/black, **low to the ground on two posts**, suburban ones w/ cul-de-sac icons
Pole warning stickers: **man getting zapped by lightning** (Ireland: bolt only + second outlined sticker)
Pedestrian crossings: **B&W striped poles w/ yellow globe** (Belisha beacons) instead of signs (also Ireland)
Rural metal gates w/ **square corners** (Ireland + Northern Ireland: rounded!)
Countryside: very green, **hedgerows**, low rolling hills (Ireland looks the same — use other clues)
**Terraced & semi-detached houses** (61% of population — Europe-top w/ IE+NL); red brick; maze-like suburbs w/ cul-de-sacs + manicured lawns; chimneys w/ **multiple round pots**; B&W timber framing
Gen4 **smallcam** coverage (low mount, big blur)

**Country flags** (England/Scotland/Wales/NI) narrow the region; **A/B-road first digit = zone** (1–8 also in NI); area codes chaotic — remember city codes: London 020, all NI 028; urban **bins carry the city name**
**Scottish Highlands**: semi-barren mountains (spring Gen4 = brown/yellow); mountainous Wales/N-England can look similar; **Lake District**: hilly + exposed rock + stone walls (rockier than Scotland)
**Scotland**: bulky red-white bollards w/ wrap-around reflector (rare elsewhere; French lookalike is spiked, Scottish round on top); diamond **'Passing Place'** signs
**Welsh** (bilingual signs, LL/D/W/Y; road marking **ARAF** = Wales-only); **Scottish Gaelic** (bh/dh/mh clusters, green/yellow sign text — Highlands, N of Glasgow)
**Scotland stone**: granite → Aberdeen; red sandstone → Glasgow/Dumfries; yellow sandstone → Edinburgh; **Cornish palms** → far SW coast + Jersey
**Northern Ireland**: black lamppost stickers w/ yellow text; **wrap-around pole warning stickers** NI-specific; western NI street signs w/ green townland text (metal divider in the north)
**Territories** (count as UK, own pages here): Isle of Man & Jersey (Gen2 only), Gibraltar, Bermuda, Falklands, South Georgia, Pitcairn, BIOT

**Street-sign city metas**: London = name black + **postcode & borough in red**; Aberdeen(shire) = blue w/ white letters (centre: black-yellow); Wakefield = black w/ white outline (Rockwell Nova font)

**London**: some Gen4 by a white **EV** w/ distinct back (same car: Dublin, Germany); **Birmingham**: yellow tape/paint + yellow stickers on most poles & lampposts; **Glasgow**: green-bordered '**Twenty's Plenty**' speed signs

Stereotypical round: often **overcast**, open grasslands w/ **gentle hills** (subtle, never mountains), scattered tree plantations, roads in worse shape than the neighbours'; completely flat → think Argentina
Plates **plain white w/ black characters**

shared_with.note: Argentine white plates have a black dot or blue top bar.

hints.text: Only **Gen3**: black car w/o antenna (not always visible); camera often **smudgy**

shared_with.note: Black Gen3 car there too — smudge much rarer.
shared_with.diff: Smudgy lens → lean Uruguay; Gen4 → Argentina.

hints.text: **Thick white wooden signposts**, often w/ up to 4 rectangular notches at the back
**Triple road lines**: double yellow w/ white dashes in between — seen nowhere else in the Americas (also: yellow-white like AR, or plain white)
**White bollards** (one side often yellow) + stone barriers — specific to Uruguay
**Trident poles** (3 upward insulators); also round poles w/ flat metal-sheet tops + **square concrete poles** — all good Uruguay clues

shared_with.note: Similar tridents in Rio Grande do Sul.

hints.text: Guardrails w/ yellow/red reflectors like Argentina — but the support has **3 sides + corner bits of a 4th** (Argentina: just 3 sides)
**Green-painted stone km marker** — unique to Uruguay; chevrons yellow-black (Argentina = white-red)
**Reddish soil** (light pink → deep red) in many areas — a good Uruguay-vs-Argentina clue (absence proves nothing)
Houses **cube-shaped concrete**, drab weathered colours, flat roofs (Argentina similar); short **stocky palms** occasionally; **cattle everywhere** (4:1 vs people)
Coverage limited: densest around **Montevideo**, elsewhere mostly yellow roads → **angle-guessing is powerful**

**Region reads**: tallest hills near **Minas** (exposed rocks + heavy bush) and **north of Tacuarembó** (darker soil, no rocks, plantations — esp. Route 30 near Tranqueras); tree-plantation hotspots north + northwest; **flat** → Laguna Merín/Brazil border SE; divided highways only near Montevideo (Ruta 1 to Colonia Valdense, Ruta IB to Punta del Este, Ruta 5 to Canelones); bins w/ orange lids → Rocha
**Montevideo** = the only really big, dense urban area (half the population); **Punta del Este** = resort town w/ modern high-rises + sandy beach, villa suburbs w/ tall pines

**Special coverage**: Isla de Flores trekker (abandoned buildings + lighthouse); **Cabo Polonio** = unique car w/ orange strap, makeshift shacks, beach driving; **Valle del Lunarejo** = white-front car w/ black curved back + brown strap on a grassy track
US territory chain (**counts as US**); the only island w/ pinpointable coverage is the **Midway Atoll** — find it slightly west of the 'North Pacific Ocean' map label
Midway coverage = **Gen2 camera**
**Albatrosses on almost every Midway location** — the standout tell (only the runways can be albatross-free)
The other islands: **Gen3 trekker coverage**, low bushy vegetation, numerous birds
**Midway runways**: nicely paved W–E on the south side; poorly paved N–S (worse northward); small **village on the eastern side**

**Other islands**: Southeast Island = small central lake + birds in the sky; Lisianski = shore-only coverage ringed by large bushes; Laysan = the only nearly cloudless coverage; French Frigate Shoals = long thin island, shabby runway, by far the most birds; East Island = smallest, barely vegetation, ocean in all directions

The **western corner of the Midway runway** is one of the most common locations on the official GeoGuessr world map — memorise it; Eastern Island (next to Midway) has only two patches of low-quality **tripod coverage**
US overseas territory (**counts as US**) — and the **only part of the US that drives on the left**
hints.uniq_note: Left-hand traffic + US signage = USVI.

hints.text: Car coverage shows a **large visible Street View car — red or white**
Architecture blend of **European Renaissance, Spanish Baroque, Rococo & Victorian**: large arched facades, central plazas, courtyards
Former **Danish colony** → Danish street/locality names (e.g. **'gade'**) + bilingual signs in historic downtowns
Plates short, **bright-blue or dark-blue** design
**St. Croix** (south): primary roads in a loose grid — main roads WSW-ENE, cross roads NNW-SSE; **St. John & St. Thomas** (north): much curvier roads
**Car = island**: red truck w/ red cab → only St. Croix (most coverage); white car WITHOUT antenna → St. Croix (rarer); white car WITH antenna + shorter cab → only St. Thomas/St. John
Two **boat trekkers** off the north coast of St. Croix: blue waters + hilly islands; near Buck Island NP the camera has a distinct **pink tint**

videos.title: Never Mix Up The US and Canada Again
How to tell USA & Canada apart
Asking GeoGuessr Devs Their Best USA Tips

hints.text: Speed signs say **"SPEED LIMIT"** in **miles** — numbers lower than Canada's, often ending in 5

shared_with.note: Canada writes 'MAXIMUM' and uses km/h (higher numbers).

hints.text: US signs are **wordy**: railway crossings literally say 'RAILROAD CROSSING'; warning text sits ON the yellow diamond

shared_with.note: Canadian crossbucks are typically blank; warning words go on a small rectangle BELOW a diagram diamond.

hints.text: **American spelling** (center, color) + **imperial units**: miles, feet & inches on bridge-height signs

shared_with.note: English Canada uses British spelling (centre, colour) and metric.

hints.text: Yellow triangle **'No Passing Zone'** signs are exclusive to the US
**Rectangular junction signs** w/ single/double arrow
Yield signs: red border, white centre **+ the word 'YIELD'**
hints.uniq_note: Canadian yield signs look identical but have NO text — text = US.

hints.text: One-way signs: black rectangle w/ white arrow, always **'ONE WAY'** printed (cities: white vertical version)

shared_with.note: Same shape/colour, but may lack the 'ONE WAY' text.

hints.text: White & orange **fibre-optic cable markers** along roads
hints.uniq_note: Almost unique to the US — very rarely seen in Canada.

hints.text: **Yellow middle + white outer lines**; double yellow middle most common; on divided roads the line nearest the median is yellow

shared_with.note: Double middle lines are less common in Canada, but not rare — caution.

hints.text: **Rumble strips** (small perpendicular indentations) on interstate/US-highway edges & between lanes

shared_with.note: Rumble strips exist in Canada but are much rarer.

hints.text: **Mobile/trailer homes** popular in rural areas (most common Southeast, found everywhere)

shared_with.note: Seen in Canada to a much lesser extent.

hints.text: **Baptist churches** very common (esp. Southern Plains & Southeast, but in all 50 states)

shared_with.note: Baptist churches are rare in Canada.

hints.text: Highway exits: large green metal signs w/ **curved edges**; blue **services signs** (food/lodging/gas) before exits

shared_with.note: Canadian highway signs similar but typically square-edged; services signs on some highways too.

hints.text: **Gen2 is rare** in the US → a North American Gen2 round is more likely Canada (exceptions: Alaska, some Hawaii, rarely Deep South); Gen3 less common than in Canada; Gen3 **side-mirror blur = single peak** (Canada: two peaks)
Parking signs: white metal w/ red/green/black text, **wordy** rules + arrows

shared_with.note: Similar style but less wordy, more symbols.

hints.text: Orange **construction barrels** w/ horizontal white stripes + black rubber base
**North America vs Europe tells** (US + Canada):
bullets: Post mailboxes w/ little side flag
Wooden utility poles + **large cylindrical metal transformers** (never in Europe)
Slightly raised insulator on top of simple poles
**Short licence plates**, no blue strip
Long metal bars w/ stoplights hanging over intersections
Thin metal signposts w/ small holes or U-profile
Long-nose semi-trucks (Europe/Asia = flat-front cabovers)
Tall water towers, sometimes w/ town name painted on
Gen3 usually has **no antenna** (US national parks: stubby coiled antenna — never in Europe)
Gen4 'smallcam' exists: low camera, big round blur, right mirror sticking out

hints.text: **Population density**: east of the Missouri River much denser than the west (clickable map)
Each state has a **unique plate design** (some readable through blur); about **half the states don't require a front plate** — rear-only skews South & East (map)
**Interstates**: even = W–E (lowest along Gulf/Mexico), odd = S–N (lowest at West Coast). **US Highways** numbered roughly inversely (low odd = East Coast, low even = north) — more exceptions there
Phone numbers start w/ a 3-digit **area code** — not clustered, but learnable (map)
Each state flies its own **state flag** — the distinct ones appear often (overview)

**Topography**: high west (Rockies, plateaus; low Central & Imperial Valley in CA) vs low east (Appalachians the main relief; hills also in the Driftless Area + Ozarks); **forest density**: east & north much denser than west/southwest
**Landscape regions**:
bullets: Great Plains (Canada→central TX): flat, few trees — fields or dry grass
Appalachians: low forested mountains, beech & oak; southern houses lower, New England style toward the north
Rockies (MT→northern NM): jagged rocky peaks, pines, year-round snow on top; some dry parts
Great Basin (NV core): tall (snow-capped) mountains behind dry grassy/shrubby plains — gentler rocky hills in OR, greener in ID/central UT
Deep South (OK→SC): reddish soil (yellow→deep red)
Prairie potholes (MT/SK border→western Great Lakes): marshy grass + small shallow ponds
Blue Ridge (S-NY→S-VA): forested rolling ridges SW–NE + flat valleys
Lower Mississippi Valley: very flat, open, red soil, corn (TN/AR/MS/NE-LA)
Ouachita Mtns (E-OK→W-AR): steeper than the nearby Ozarks
Four Corners (UT/AZ/NM/CO): red mesas & tablelands + red soil
Pacific coast (WA/OR/N-CA): temperate rainforest — broadleaf + huge conifers + ferns
Eastern WA (+N/E OR): golden agricultural fields
CA Central Valley: Mediterranean — vineyards, olives, rolling edges
TX Hill Country (W of Austin/San Antonio): dry rocky hills, yucca, prickly pear, live oaks
Rio Grande Valley (S-TX): extremely flat, shrubby trees + fields, Tamaulipas border

hints.text: **Crops**:
bullets: Corn: Midwest corn belt (see map)
Soybeans (short dark-green rows): Eastern Plains, Midwest, Lower Mississippi, Mid-Atlantic
Canola (yellow flowers): North Dakota along the Canadian border (rarely MT)
Cotton (fluffy white): coastal SE, Lower Mississippi, W-TX, parts of CA/AZ
Rice (flat low prairies): ONLY north-central CA + Lower Mississippi (AR/LA/TX/MS) — great region tell

hints.text: **Flora**:
bullets: Eastern White Pine (layered branches curving up): New England, northern Midwest, Appalachians
Southern pines (tall, fluffy needle clusters): the Southeast signature tree
Southern lawns (Bermuda/St. Augustine grass) turn beige in winter; northern lawns stay green
Sagebrush (blue-green shrub): arid interior West
Douglas firs (extremely tall, dark): Cascadia + N-CA/ID/MT (also BC!)
Fan palms: SE coasts + desert Southwest (Mexican Fan Palm: CA + Gulf Coast)
Spanish moss (grey-white drapes on trees): Deep South

hints.text: **State infrastructure IDs**: each state has its own **highway shield** + distinct **Adopt-a-Highway** sign designs; blue-yellow **county road shields** in certain states; **US National Parks paint guardrails/signs/posts brown** (wood or metal, sometimes rock walls)
**Road-surface state metas**:
bullets: Rectangular raised pavement markers (RPMs): south + west + Massachusetts
Snowplowable 'H'-shaped markers: eastern highway states only
RPMs BESIDE the double yellow (not between): GA, FL, TX, CA
Extra-wide gap between yellow lines: TX, AR, LA, MS, OR
Grainy 'salt & pepper' surface: Texas (also nearby NM/OK/CO)
Grainy rural roads in the Upper Midwest: most common Illinois
Black stripe between the yellows: California
No middle line, white edges only: small roads in CA & KY
Thicker yellow middle lines: WV, MD, DE, VA

hints.text: **Multi-state sign metas**:
bullets: Signs on TWO posts: MI, OH, MN, NY (most common)
Coloured driveway signs w/ house number + town/road name: WI & MI peninsula (neighbours: numbers only)
White intersection signs: Virginia (and North Carolina)
Black street signs w/ white text: South Carolina
Green/red bridge & guardrail end markers: ME, NH, MA
NY/VT small green signs w/ 3 number rows: VT 2nd row starts 0; NY 2nd row = NYSDOT district

hints.text: **New England kit**: short roadside **rock walls** (CT/RI/MA/NH); coastal **shingle-wall** houses; urban **three-deckers** (NY/CT/RI/MA — also Atlantic Canada); **brownstone** row houses (NE cities + Chicago)
**Architecture by region**:
bullets: Stilt houses: southern Atlantic & Gulf coasts
Short concrete/wood stilts (1–3 ft): western/central Gulf Coast
Very large sloped roofs: OK, TX, LA
Red metal roofs: Mid-South & southern Appalachia (VA/WV/OH/KY/TN/AR/NC)
Spanish Colonial Revival (light stucco + red clay tiles): desert SW (CA/NV/AZ/TX) + FL/coastal GA
Dark wooden boards + metal roofs: interior mountain West

hints.text: **Pole metas**: 3 yellow base stripes → most of California (exc. Modoc/Lassen; also Grants Pass, OR); white plate w/ orange rectangle + diamond → Wisconsin & Michigan's Upper Peninsula; yellow **PacifiCorp** plates → OR, E-ID, UT, WY (rarer CA/WA)
**Store & restaurant chains** (region deciders):
bullets: US-only gas & food chains exist that Canada lacks (see graphics; Chick-fil-A the near-only exception)
ShopRite: CT/NY/NJ/PA/DE/MD · Hy-Vee: IA/MN/WI/IL · Piggly Wiggly: SE + WI
H-E-B: Texas only · Publix: FL + Deep South · Whataburger: TX-centred South
Waffle House: South/SE (GA #1) · Zaxby's: SE (GA #1, pockets in UT/MT)
Carl's Jr. = West Coast/SW vs Hardee's = central/East (same logo! Both only in WY & OK)
Pizza Ranch: IA/MN · In-N-Out: SW (2/3 in CA) · Tim Hortons (US side): near ON/QC border
WaWa: Mid-Atlantic + FL (NJ/Philly densest) · Sheetz: PA/Mid-Atlantic/Appalachia
Kwik Trip: WI/MN ('Kwik Star' in IA) · Kum & Go: IA + Ozarks/CO/WY/ND · Casey's: Midwest/Plains (IL/IA/MO) · Buc-ee's (oversized stations): TX

hints.text: **Non-contiguous US** (all have their own pages on this site): Alaska (northern-/western-/easternmost state), Hawaii (only state outside N. America), Puerto Rico (Caribbean Commonwealth), American Samoa, Guam, Northern Marianas, US Virgin Islands (**the only left-driving part of the US**), US Minor Outlying Islands (Midway)
**Saguaro cacti** (tall tubes w/ branching arms) → southern Arizona, Sonoran border
Utility poles w/ **3 yellow stripes** at the base (most of CA, exc. Modoc/Lassen NE)
hints.uniq_note: Also in Grants Pass, Oregon — otherwise a CA lock.

hints.text: **Front Range**: N–S mountain ridge flanking Denver/Boulder/Colorado Springs — Rockies west, Great Plains east
Central FL (Orlando↔Lakeland) = the only real **rolling hills** area, w/ lakes + fields
**Square-reflector bollard** (also UT/AZ) — Idaho appears w/ both 1 and 2 bolts (UT usually 2, AZ 1)
Grainy **salt-and-pepper rural roads** of the Upper Midwest are most common in Illinois (colour varies)
**Concrete paving** on ordinary rural roads much more common than elsewhere (ignore bridges/highways/cities)
hints.uniq_note: Only trust it on plain rural roads.

hints.text: **White reflectors on signposts** (esp. rural stop signs) — unique to Kansas
Uniquely-shaped **rumble strips**: thin, long recessed lines on the very outside road edges
**Shotgun houses** (very narrow 1-storey wood) → New Orleans area (also Houston, Louisville, Mississippi)
Stoplights & signs **hang from wires** across big intersections, often in yellow boxes; signs often on two posts (also OH/MN/NY)
hints.uniq_note: Not exclusive, but by far most common in Michigan.

hints.text: **Yellow signs w/ vertical black triangles** before bridges & guardrail ends; horizontal black-white marker on rural stop-sign posts
County highways lettered **A–ZZZ** (only WI does this too)
hints.uniq_note: Wisconsin usually writes 'COUNTY' above the letter — Missouri doesn't.

hints.text: **Sandhills**: rolling prairie-grass dunes in the central-north of the state
Bollard = **white vertical-rectangle reflector w/ black outline** on a thin stick
White metal **'E-2' sign** beside the road, typically at bridge ends
Bollard w/ **3 circular reflectors**; **Pueblo-Revival adobe** houses (rounded edges, flat roofs, sand-brown — also AZ/CO to a lesser extent)
**Square guardrails** (long top bar on square legs) dominant (also Wyoming); small green 3-row number signs (2nd row = NYSDOT district; Vermont's start w/ 0)
Street signs showing **street numbers + arrows** alongside the name; plain white signs on posts common too
**Green sign w/ 3 rows of numbers** on the back of rural stop signs
Speed signs often just say **'SPEED'**; bollard = very long rectangular reflector on top (similar in CA)
hints.uniq_note: 'SPEED'-only wording is an Oregon habit.

hints.text: White rectangular signs at intersections w/ the **converging state-route number** on top
**Black street signs w/ white text** common at intersections
**White diamond-reflector bollard**, usually **2 bolts** (Montana = 1 bolt; design exists elsewhere — caution, squares can be rotated)
**Yellow/red 'ketchup & mustard' bands** around signposts; grainy salt-and-pepper roads; huge sloped roofs (also OK/LA); oil pumpjacks (also E-NM/ND)
Bollard = **white square reflector atop a black rectangle** (unique to Utah); black paddle bollards (also WY/NE); streets named like **'600 South'** (also ID)
Small **green 3-row number signs** — 2nd row almost always starts w/ **0** (New York's = NYSDOT district number)
hints.uniq_note: Same sign type as NY — the 0 is the Vermont tell.

hints.text: White rectangular signs at intersections; **thicker yellow middle lines** (also WV/MD/DE)
Bollard = white/brown post, **white rectangle front + two small squares on the back**; golden wheat fields in the east
Street signs w/ a **fraction-in-a-circle** symbol
Simple **white bollards w/ black reflectors** (unique); green **ATV Route** signs (unique); orange rectangle+diamond pole plate (also MI UP); county letters w/ the word 'COUNTY'
Bollard: dark post, white circular reflector w/ the post rising **above** it — top sometimes painted **white/grey (unique)**; black paddle bollard w/ white square reflector = WY-only variant

**Landscape spotlights**: Everglades (jungle-straight lonely roads Ft. Myers↔Miami); Florida Keys (single-lane highway, tropical green, white sandy soil); Wichita Mtns (SW-OK, pink-red rocky peaks); Guadalupe Mtns (NM/TX border, tall dry ridge); Black Hills (W-SD/E-WY, conifer mountains in flat surroundings)
**Ford Bronco** (unblurred black roof, red hood, 2 escorting Ford SUVs behind) on unpaved rural roads = TransAmerica-Trail promo coverage, Oregon → North Carolina
**Interstate 19** (Tucson → Mexico border) is the only US highway signed in **kilometres** (speed limits still MPH)

**City tells**: Pittsburgh = yellow steel bridges + 3 rivers + bluffs; San Francisco = colourful Victorian row houses w/ bay windows; New Orleans = colourful shotgun houses + wrap-around balconies; Miami (Beach) = bright art-deco blocks near the coast
**National parks**:
bullets: Dry Tortugas (W of Key West): hexagonal brick fort + moat; Loggerhead lighthouse brown/white
Big Bend (SW-TX): dry rocky peaks, yucca + prickly pear (Chihuahuan desert)
Death Valley: dry sandy treeless mountains, dusty Gen3 sky
Glacier (MT/Canada border): extremely tall steep peaks, hazy summer coverage
Badlands (SD): sand-brown eroded formations
Theodore Roosevelt (ND): same but w/ more trees + fall colours
Devils Tower (NE-WY): lone giant butte visible for miles

hints.text: Pacific island nation NE of Brisbane; all coverage = **Gen3 trekker**
Volcanic island → **dark volcanic soil**; tropical, much coverage in dense forest
Of 13 islands, only **Ambrym Island** has coverage (find the 'Port Vato' label, N of Port Vila); a unique volcano trekker is in the centre
E of the volcano: a plateau w/ dark brown soil, then jungle w/ ferns (man in a red cap), then a **black-sand beach** on the east coast
**Endu Pahakol** = the only village w/ coverage — small **thatched huts** + dark soil
Drives on the **right**

shared_with.note: Also right-hand traffic with normal Street View.
shared_with.diff: Khmer script → Cambodia; Vietnamese = Latin w/ heavy diacritics.

hints.text: Plates **white with black lettering** — long and short versions
**Vietnamese**: Latin script with **more diacritics than anything else on Street View**; words short, mostly single-syllable
Most common bollard: small **square concrete, white with red top**
Sign posts painted in a **red-and-white stripe pattern**; road signs have a **yellow-orange background** (unique within SE Asia)
hints.uniq_note: The yellow-orange sign background is unique within Southeast Asia — outside the region confirm with the diacritic-heavy language.

hints.text: **Waystones white with colourful (red/blue) top** — road number on the side, distances front/back (great for pinpointing)

shared_with.note: Similar white waystones with coloured tops.

hints.text: Chevrons **black-on-yellow or white-on-red**, typically with only ONE arrow; directional signs blue w/ thin/no border and oddly-angled white arrows
Distinctly **short yellow middle dashes**; minor roads of **concrete blocks** very common
**A-type guardrails** with reflectors **red on one side / yellow on the other**; red-white striped concrete barriers on mountain roads
hints.uniq_note: The two-coloured (red/yellow) guardrail reflectors are very recognisable — standard A-type guardrails elsewhere use a single colour.

hints.text: **Holey poles** (holes on lower half or nearly full length)

shared_with.note: The only other Asian country with common holey poles — but the holes are **much larger**.
shared_with.diff: Big holes + Sinhala script → Sri Lanka.

hints.text: Round concrete poles with **indents + small pin holes** near the top; upside-down **trapezoid poletops**; **double poles**; light-grey **electricity boxes** (1–4 indented windows, bottom sloping down)
**Motorcycle coverage** (bone-shaped blur, one side higher; sometimes a visible helmet) — Gen3 + Gen4; by far the most in the world
hints.uniq_note: Motorbike coverage exists elsewhere, but rural motorbike coverage almost guarantees Vietnam — no other country has anywhere near this much.

hints.text: **Shitcam** exists in a few spots — with the same bone-shaped blur (other shitcam countries have a bigger circular blur); only in parts of the **Red River Delta**
**Grain drying on roads**
**Addresses on storefronts** run specific→general: number → ward/commune (phường/xã) → district (quận/huyện/thị xã/thành phố) → province (tỉnh) — often abbreviated (H., TP., TB.)
**Provinces** = the key address part — learn names + abbreviations (TG = Tien Giang)
bullets: 2025 reform merged most provinces — coverage predates it, so OLD province names are NOT scannable on the map → memorise them

hints.text: **Area codes** start with 2 (+1–2 digits), ordered north → south; **plate codes** (often painted on truck sides) map to regions; some **place-name prefixes** are regional

**Red River Delta**: wide, completely flat fields (less commonly Mekong); single **graves in the middle of fields**; boxy tall multi-storey houses with **Greco-Roman kitsch** (pillars, pediments — sometimes near HCMC too)
**Mekong Delta**: extremely flat & water-rich, dead-straight canals with linear villages, dense population, even more tropical (palms, wetland); houses boxy but **single-storey**
**Central Highlands**: hilly w/ distant mountains, **red soil**, dry rolling fields, drier vegetation; **black-pepper plantations** (vines shrouding tree trunks) in/around the highlands

**Crop metas**: rubber plantations (thin trunks w/ plastic wrap, dense grid) → north of HCMC + Central Highlands; tea (trimmed rows on hills) → north + Bảo Lộc/Lâm Đồng; khasi-pine clusters → far NE (Lạng Sơn, Quảng Ninh); dragonfruit (wet-mop look) → Phan Thiết + Long An/Tiền Giang border

Coast NE of Phan Thiết: noticeably **dry, sandy, partly rocky**; **wind-turbine clusters** mostly in the south (SE of Dalat, N of Buon Ma Thuot, S of Pleiku, SE of Dong Hoi, E of Quy Nhon, offshore S of HCMC, W-Quang Tri)
**North**: thin roof spires (almost only northern half); NW: traditional **wooden stilt houses** w/ corrugated roofs + triangular roof openings; grid-pattern **stick/bamboo fences** (like NE India)

**Pole region metas**: full-length holey poles → north (half-length = everywhere); few/no pinholes → north; trapezoid poletops → centre+north (3 off-centre incomplete trapezoids → north); triangle poletop w/ supports under crossbar → south; A-shape above crossbar → centre; uneven/off-centre crossbars → south; bamboo poles → rural NW mountains; black pole stickers (Malaysia-like, simpler) → north
Countryside densely covered in **greenhouses**
**Đồng Văn Karst Geopark** (far north): rounded pointy mountains w/ sparse vegetation + exposed **dark rock** (the wider north has pointy mountains too — the exposed rock is the tell)
Island near Cambodia: **Gen3 only**, dark red soil, forested ridge in the NE
Coastal roads east of Hải Phòng: **pointy rocky islands** rising from the sea
A **single large mountain** in an otherwise perfectly flat landscape (NE of the city)
Northern peninsula: **sand dunes hugging the road** (mostly divided highway)
Historic town south of Da Nang: **old yellow buildings** with dark brown doors/details
Mountain resort town: touristy stores, excessive signage, **palace-like grand hotels** (European-SEA style mix)
Beach resorts east of the city: signs with **Cyrillic** alongside Vietnamese (Russian tourists)
